16-19. This scenario relates to four requirements.
It is 1 July 20X5. You are an audit supervisor with Oogway & Co, which has recently
been appointed as auditor to Lotus Blossom Co (Lotus), and you are planning your first
audit which will be for the year ending 31 July 20X5. Lotus develops and manufactures
electrical components.
The audit engagement partner had a meeting with Crane Wing, Lotus’ finance director,
and has advised you of the following:
Inventory is valued at the lower of cost and net realisable value. Cost is made up of the
purchase price of raw materials and costs of conversion, including labour and
production overheads. The finance director has reviewed the inventory valuation policy
and has decided to include some additional overheads this year as they consider them
to be production related.
Inventory is held in two main warehouses close to the manufacturing facility. The
company plans to conduct full inventory counts at the warehouses on 29 and 30 July,
and any necessary adjustments will be made post year end to reflect movements of
inventory occurring on 31 July.
During the year, whilst calculating depreciation, the directors extended the useful lives
of plant and machinery from three years to five years.
The company receives a trade discount from one of its main suppliers, Bamboo Co,
which is linked to the total amount purchased during the year. The amount is reclaimed
post year end in September 20X5 and is based on the actual volumes purchased during
the year ended 31 July 20X5. During the last month it has become clear that Lotus will
reach the volume required to reclaim this discount. The finance director has advised
that they are expecting to include a receivable in relation to this trade discount of $0·9m
at the year end along with a corresponding entry in cost of sales, as the discount relates
to goods which will have all been sold by the year end.
The directors need to meet a target profit before tax of $1.5 million in order to be paid
their annual bonus.
In May 20X5 the financial controller of Lotus was dismissed. He had been employed by
the company for over 20 years, and he has threatened to sue the company for unfair
dismissal. The role of financial controller has not yet been filled and so his tasks have
been shared between the existing finance department team. In addition, the purchase
ledger supervisor left in March and a replacement has been appointed in the last week.
However, for this period, no supplier statement reconciliations were performed.
Lotus’ payroll function is outsourced to an external service organisation, Harmony Co,
which is responsible for all elements of payroll processing and maintenance of payroll
records.
(a) Describe EIGHT audit risks and explain the auditor’s response to each risk
in planning the audit of Lotus Co.
(16 marks)
(b) Describe substantive procedures the auditor should perform to obtain
sufficient and appropriate audit evidence in relation to Lotus Co’s
directors’ bonuses.
(5 marks)
It is now 2 November 20X5 and the audit of Lotus Co has been completed. The
auditor’s report was signed in October and, in line with Oogway & Co’s quality
management procedures for new clients, a post-issuance review has been carried out.
During the review of Lotus Co’s audit files, the engagement quality reviewer noted the
following:
During the audit, the audit team became aware of a breach of data protection
regulation, whereby an employee of the company had made its customer database
available to a third party. This disclosure is in contravention of the regulation. The audit
working papers refer briefly to this situation and conclude that ‘it has little to do with the
audit, as no one outside of the company is aware’. No further investigations were made
by the audit team, and the audit manager noted in the working papers that ‘the matter
does not need to be discussed any further with Lotus Co’s management team as I have
received assurance that the person responsible for the breach of regulation has been
dismissed.’
To reduce sample sizes used in tests of detail, the samples were selected based on
judgement rather than statistical methods. In addition, only parts of the population being
tested were sampled, for example, certain locations were not included in the sample of
non-current assets selected for physical verification.
(c) Identify and explain the quality management deficiencies in the approach
adopted by Oogway & Co.
(5 marks)
ISA 260 Communication with Those Charged with Governance provides guidance to
auditors in relation to communicating with those charged with governance on matters
arising from the audit of an entity’s financial statements.
(d)
(i) Explain why it is important for auditors to communicate throughout the
audit with those charged with governance; and
(ii) Identify TWO examples of matters which the auditor may communicate to
those charged with governance.
Note: The total marks will be split equally between each part.
(4 marks)
(30 marks)
Syllabus reference: C2, C3 & A3
20-22.This scenario relates to two requirements.
It is 1 July 20X5. VistaCraft Co (VistaCraft) manufactures double glazed windows and
doors and is planning to list on a stock exchange within the next nine months.
The company's year end is 30 September 20X5. You are an audit supervisor with
Crystal & Co, and you are in the process of reviewing the following extracts from the
internal controls documentation in preparation for the forthcoming audit:
Bank and cash
On a weekly basis, a bank payments list is generated for supplier payments. The
finance director reviews the total amount of the bank payments list and authorises it.
She then passes it to the financial controller who processes it for payment.
VistaCraft incurs a lot of petty cash expenditure and the finance department maintains a
petty cash float of $750 which is kept in the safe. It is used for making any sundry
purchases by the company. When staff wish to purchase sundry items, the required
sum of cash is given to the staff member who signs for it. The staff member is required
to return any excess money to the finance department but there is currently no
requirement for receipts to be provided.
VistaCraft maintains four bank accounts. The cashier reconciles the main current
account on a monthly basis as this contains the highest levels of activity and reconciles
the remaining three bank accounts every three months. The reconciliations are
reviewed by the finance director who evidences this review.
Payroll
VistaCraft employs a mixture of factory staff, who work a standard shift of eight hours a
day, and administration and sales staff. All staff are paid monthly by bank transfer.
Occasionally, overtime is required of factory staff. Where this occurs, details of overtime
worked per employee is collated and submitted to the payroll department by a
production clerk. The payroll department pays this overtime in the month it occurs. At
the end of each quarter, the company’s payroll department sends overtime reports
which detail the amount of overtime worked to the production director for their review.
VistaCraft’s payroll package produces a list of payments per employee which links into
the bank system to produce a list of automatic bank transfer payments. The finance
director reviews the total to be paid on the list of automatic payments and compares this
to the total payroll amount to be paid for the month per the payroll records. If any issues
arise, then the automatic bank transfer can be manually changed by the finance
director.
(a) Describe THREE matters the auditor may consider in determining whether a
deficiency in internal control is significant.
(3 marks)
(b) In respect of the systems of internal control of VistaCraft Co:
(i)  Identify and explain FOUR deficiencies;
(ii)  Recommend a control to address each of these deficiencies; and
(iii) Describe a TEST OF CONTROL the external auditors should perform to
assess if each of these controls, if implemented, is operating effectively.
Note: The total marks will be split equally between each part.
(12 marks)
(c)
VistaCraft is considering the need for compliance with corporate governance provisions
due to their plans to list on a stock exchange.
In particular, the finance director is looking to recruit non-executive directors as she
understands that VistaCraft will need to establish an audit committee. The finance
director has two potential non-executive directors whom she is considering approaching
to join the board of VistaCraft.
Shimmer Pane is currently an executive sales director of a listed multi-national banking
company; she sits on an audit committee of another company as a non-executive
director and is agreeable to being paid a fixed fee which is not related to profits.
David Shine is currently a finance director of a small manufacturing company, which
does not compete with VistaCraft; he has expressed an interest in a fixed seven year
contract and he is the brother of VistaCraft’s chief executive.
Required:
Discuss the advantages and disadvantages of appointing Shimmer Pane and
David Shine as non-executive directors of VistaCraft Co.
(5 marks)
(20 marks)
Syllabus reference: D4, D5 & E5
23-26.This scenario relates to four requirements.
It is 1 July 20X5. Arctic Co (Arctic) manufactures and distributes fashion clothing to retail
stores. Its draft financial statements show total assets of $65.4m and profit before tax of
$8.9m.
Your firm, Frostline & Co, is about to commence the final audit for the year ended 31
May 20X5.
The following matters have been brought to your attention
Bank reconciliation
During last year’s audit of Arctic’s bank and cash, significant cut off errors were
discovered with a number of post year-end cheques being processed prior to the year
end to reduce payables. The finance director has assured the audit engagement partner
that this error has not occurred again this year and that the bank reconciliation has been
carefully prepared. The audit engagement partner has asked that the bank
reconciliation is comprehensively audited.
Revaluation of property, plant and equipment (PPE)
At the beginning of the year, management undertook an extensive review of Arctic’s
non-current asset valuations and as a result decided to update the carrying value of all
PPE. The finance director contacted his brother, Henry, who is a valuer and requested
that Henry’s firm undertake the valuation, which took place in June 20X4.
Trade payables and accruals
Arctic purchases its raw materials from a large number of suppliers. The company’s
policy is to close the purchases general ledger just after the year end and the financial
controller is responsible for identifying goods which were received pre-year-end but for
which no invoice has yet been received. An accrual is calculated for goods received but
not yet invoiced (GRNI) and is included within trade payables and accruals.
The audit strategy has identified a risk over the completeness of trade payables and
accruals. The audit team will utilise automated tools and techniques in the form of audit
software while auditing trade payables and accruals.
Requirements
(a) Describe substantive procedures the auditor should perform to obtain
sufficient and appropriate audit evidence in relation to the bank
reconciliation.
(5 marks)
(b) Describe substantive procedures the auditor should perform to obtain
sufficient and appropriate audit evidence in relation to the revaluation of
property, plant and equipment.
(5 marks)
(c) Describe substantive procedures the auditor should perform to obtain
sufficient and appropriate audit evidence in relation to the COMPLETENESS
of Arctic Co’s trade payables and accruals.
(5 marks)
It is now 28 August 20X5 and the audit of Arctic Co is almost complete. The auditor's
report is due to be signed shortly. The following matter has been brought to your
attention:
On 14 July 20X5, Ice Co, a customer of Arctic Co with a receivables balance of
$692,000 at 31 May 20X5, notified Arctic that it was experiencing significant cash flow
difficulties and would be unable to make any payments for the foreseeable future. The
finance director of Arctic believes that as Ice Co is a long-standing customer and has
been trading for many years, the outstanding amount will be received in full in due
course and has therefore not adjusted the receivable balance in the financial statements
for the year ended 31 May 20X5.
(d) Discuss the issue and describe the impact on the auditor’s report, if any,
should this issue remain unresolved.
(5 marks)
(20 marks)
source_doc: ACCA:aa:objective:AA  24年12月考季 官方Pre-Dec Mock Q.pdf#30

stem_zh:
1. Recuse or remove herself from the engagement
When an auditor has a close personal relationship with someone in a position to
exert influence over the financial statements at the audit client (like the CFO), it
can affect their independence and objectivity. Recusing herself means stepping
back from the audit to avoid any bias.
Why Other Answers Are Wrong:
• Maintain confidentiality and proceed with the audit:
This is incorrect because maintaining confidentiality does not address the
potential familiarity and self-interest threat.
• Accept the situation as common in the industry:
The threat to independence is significant due to the seniority of the audit
team member and the client management personnel and therefore cannot
be ignored.
• Disclose the relationship to the audit committee and seek guidance:
Although this needs to be communicated to the client’s TCWG, this is not a
sufficient safeguard in itself. The best answer is removing the auditor or
recusing herself.
source_doc: ACCA:aa:objective:AA Pre Mar 25 Mock Answers (per practice platform).pdf#1

stem_zh:
2. Report it to the appropriate level of management and those charged with
governance and request that they correct the misstatement
TCGW are usually considered independent because they are not involved in the
day-to-day running of the operations. Bringing it to their attention will ensure the
adjustment is made in the financial statements.
Why Other Answers Are Wrong:
• Ignore it, as it won’t impact the overall financial position significantly:
This is not true as the matter is material and will affect the true and fair view
status of the financial statements.
• Report it to the CFO but exclude it from the audit report:
The CFO might be the reason behind the matter and might have caused it.
Even if they are not, since there is a fear that this might affect the stock
price negatively, the CFO might be keen to want hide this matter. If no
adjustments are made, there is need for the auditor to consider how the
material misstatement could influence their report.
• Adjust the financial statements to reflect the accurate revenue:
The audit team should not do the adjustment of the financial statements.
Rather, they can instruct management to do so to reflect the accuracy in
revenue but they themselves cannot do this, as it will bring about a self-
review and threat.
source_doc: ACCA:aa:objective:AA Pre Mar 25 Mock Answers (per practice platform).pdf#2

stem_zh: 3. 1 & 3 only
source_doc: ACCA:aa:objective:AA Pre Mar 25 Mock Answers (per practice platform).pdf#3

stem_zh:
1. Self-interest threat: This occurs when the auditor could benefit, financially or
otherwise, from a financial outcome of the subject matter of the audit. In this
case, accepting a lavish dinner could be seen as a personal benefit that might
influence their independence.
source_doc: ACCA:aa:objective:AA Pre Mar 25 Mock Answers (per practice platform).pdf#4

stem_zh:
2. Self-Review threat: This is not applicable in this scenario. A self-review threat
would occur if the auditors were reviewing their own work, which is not the case
here.
source_doc: ACCA:aa:objective:AA Pre Mar 25 Mock Answers (per practice platform).pdf#5

stem_zh:
3. Familiarity threat: This occurs when auditors become too familiar with or
trusting of the client, potentially compromising their professional scepticism.
Accepting a lavish dinner could lead to a familiarity threat as it might create a
closer relationship between the auditors and the client.
source_doc: ACCA:aa:objective:AA Pre Mar 25 Mock Answers (per practice platform).pdf#6

stem_zh:
4. Professional behaviour: This is not a threat to independence. It is a
fundamental principle that auditors should adhere to.
source_doc: ACCA:aa:objective:AA Pre Mar 25 Mock Answers (per practice platform).pdf#7

stem_zh:
4. The use of separate teams to provide the audit and non-audit services
Using separate teams will not address the self-interest threat from the fee levels as
separating the teams will not alleviate the firm’s potential financial dependence on
Uniheart Corporation and therefore the risk that work is not carried out
independently for fear of the losing the client.
source_doc: ACCA:aa:objective:AA Pre Mar 25 Mock Answers (per practice platform).pdf#8

stem_zh:
5. The audit partner should politely inform the finance director that the team would
undertake the audit in accordance with all relevant professional standards and
quality management procedures.
The audit partner should politely inform the finance director that the team would
undertake the audit in accordance with all relevant International Standards on
Auditing (ISAs) and quality management procedures. This ensures that the audit
process is not compromised, and the integrity and objectivity of the audit are
maintained, despite the pressure from the CFO. It is important to remember that
the primary responsibility of an auditor is to the shareholders, not the
management, and the audit should be thorough and unbiased.
First option: Agreeing with the CFO and quickening the audit process to meet the
deadline is not appropriate. This could compromise the quality and thoroughness
of the audit, potentially missing significant issues. It is important for auditors to
maintain their professional scepticism and not allow management pressure to
influence their judgment.
Second option: Resigning from the audit engagement due to the pressure from the
CFO is an extreme measure. While it might be considered if the pressure
continues and compromises the auditor’s ability to perform a thorough and
unbiased audit, it should not be the first course of action. Communication and
setting clear expectations with the client about the audit process and its
requirements are crucial.
Fourth option: Reducing the size of the samples that the audit planned to test on
so that the work is completed within the required deadlines could lead to a less
thorough audit and increase the risk of not detecting material misstatements. The
sample size should be determined based on the auditor’s professional judgment
considering the risk of material misstatement, not the time constraints.
source_doc: ACCA:aa:objective:AA Pre Mar 25 Mock Answers (per practice platform).pdf#9

stem_zh:
6. Adjusting event.
The discovery of the material fraud is an adjusting event. According to
International Accounting Standard (IAS) 10, an adjusting event is one that provides
evidence of conditions that existed at the end of the reporting period. In this case,
the fraud had been ongoing for the past six months, which includes the period up
to 30 April, 20X5. Therefore, the financial statements for the year ended 30 April,
20X5 should be adjusted to reflect the fraud. This is because the conditions that
led to the fraud (i.e., the fictitious sales) were already present at the end of the
reporting period. The discovery of the fraud merely provided evidence of these
conditions. Hence, it is an adjusting event.
source_doc: ACCA:aa:objective:AA Pre Mar 25 Mock Answers (per practice platform).pdf#10

stem_zh:
7. The auditor's report must include an Emphasis of Matter paragraph that highlights a
matter that has been correctly disclosed in the financial statements
An EOMP is not mandatory element of the audit report. Auditors will use their
judgment to evaluate if the correctly presented or disclosed matters are of such
fundamental importance to users’ understanding of the financial statements that
the auditor should highlight the disclosure.
source_doc: ACCA:aa:objective:AA Pre Mar 25 Mock Answers (per practice platform).pdf#11

stem_zh:
8. The company’s ability to continue operations for the foreseeable future..
When evaluating the appropriateness of management’s assessment of the
company’s ability to continue as a going concern, an auditor should consider all
available information about the future, which is at least, but not limited to, twelve
months from the end of the reporting period. This includes information about the
company’s current and expected performance, its cash flows, its borrowing
requirements and repayment schedules, and the external environment in which it
operates.
In the given scenario, Samsingsong Corporation is facing several issues that could
impact its ability to continue as a going concern. These include a material fraud,
financial strain due to declining sales and increased competition, worsening
liquidity position, defaults on payments, and a potential lawsuit. All these factors
could affect the company’s ability to continue operations in the foreseeable future.
Therefore, these should be considered when evaluating the appropriateness of
management’s assessment on going concern. The other options (A, B, and D) are
also considerations but they are not as key as option C. Hence, option C is the
most appropriate answer.
source_doc: ACCA:aa:objective:AA Pre Mar 25 Mock Answers (per practice platform).pdf#12

stem_zh:
9. Issue an unqualified opinion with an emphasis of matter paragraph.
In this scenario, the management of Samsingsong Corporation has appropriately
disclosed the lawsuit in the notes to the financial statements. They have not made
any provision for potential liability because the outcome of the lawsuit is possible,
not probable. This is in line with the accounting standards which require that a
provision should only be made when an outflow of resources is probable (more
likely than not) and an estimate can be made reliably.
An auditor issues an unqualified opinion when the financial statements are
presented fairly in all material respects in accordance with the applicable financial
reporting framework, and there are no material misstatements. The existence of
the lawsuit does not constitute a misstatement in the financial statements, as it has
been appropriately disclosed in the notes.
However, due to the significance of the event (the lawsuit), the auditor may choose
to draw attention to it without modifying the opinion. This is done through an
emphasis of matter paragraph in the auditor's report. This paragraph refers to a
note that discloses the matter. The paragraph includes a reference to the
appropriate note, and a statement that the auditor's opinion is not modified in
respect of the matter emphasized.
The other options involve modifying the auditor's opinion, which is not appropriate
in this case as the financial statements are not materially misstated. The lawsuit
has been appropriately disclosed, and no provision is required as the outcome is
not probable. Therefore, these options are not correct.
source_doc: ACCA:aa:objective:AA Pre Mar 25 Mock Answers (per practice platform).pdf#13

stem_zh:
10. An unqualified audit opinion will be given if the management correctly and
adequately discloses the material uncertainty relating to going concern in the
financial statements and a Material Uncertainty Related to Going Concern
(MURGC) section will be added in the audit report.
An unqualified opinion is issued when the auditor concludes that the financial
statements give a true and fair view in accordance with the financial reporting
framework used for the preparation and presentation of the financial statements.
An MURGC is included in the audit report when there is a material uncertainty
regarding going concern that has been adequately disclosed in the financial
statements. The auditor uses this paragraph to draw the attention of the users to
the client’s disclosure note.
The first statement is incorrect as the audit report is modified due to the inclusion
of the MURGC.
The second statement is incorrect as modification to the audit opinion is not
required if the disclosure is correctly and adequately given.
The third statement is incorrect as an MURGC is used to highlight a correctly given
material uncertainty disclosure, not EOM.
source_doc: ACCA:aa:objective:AA Pre Mar 25 Mock Answers (per practice platform).pdf#14

stem_zh:
11. Visit the hotels and physically inspecting the newly acquired gym equipment,
renovated swimming pools, and expanded spa services.
This is because the existence assertion is concerned with whether the assets (in
this case, the upgraded leisure facilities) actually exist. Physical inspection
provides the most direct evidence of existence.
The first option: Taking a sample of assets and ensuring they have been recorded
in the asset register is more relevant to the completeness assertion, not the
existence assertion. It doesn’t confirm whether the assets recorded in the register
actually exist or not.
The second option: Comparing the current year’s leisure facilities expenditure with
the prior year’s expenditure to identify significant fluctuations cannot provide direct
evidence about the existence of the assets.
The fourth option: Review the invoices from suppliers and contractors to verify they
are in Carlos’ name is more relevant to the rights and obligations assertion.
source_doc: ACCA:aa:objective:AA Pre Mar 25 Mock Answers (per practice platform).pdf#15

stem_zh:
12. Reviewing the legal correspondence and settlement agreements related to the food
poisoning claims
This procedure would provide insights into the financial impact of the food
poisoning claims and any potential liabilities faced by Carlos Co.
The second option (Inspecting kitchen facilities and hygiene practices): This
procedure does not directly address the specific issue of food poisoning claims.
The third option (Vouching sales transactions): This procedure is relevant for
revenue testing but does not specifically address the food poisoning allegations.
The fourth option (Confirming accounts payable balances): This procedure is
unrelated to the specific issue of food poisoning claims.
Therefore, the most relevant audit procedure in this scenario is the first option
(Reviewing legal correspondence and settlement agreements).
source_doc: ACCA:aa:objective:AA Pre Mar 25 Mock Answers (per practice platform).pdf#16

stem_zh:
13. Review the system documentation to understand the design of the new system and
the controls incorporated into it and document.
This is the correct answer. Reviewing system documentation allows the auditor to
understand the design of the new system and the controls incorporated into it. This
is a crucial step in assessing both the design and operating effectiveness of the
system. The auditor can identify what controls are in place, how they are supposed
to work, and then plan further procedures to test whether these controls are
operating effectively.
The second option: Perform tests of details. Substantive procedures are performed
to detect material misstatements at the assertion level. They are not designed to
assess the design and operating effectiveness of a system’s controls.
The third option: Conduct Physical Verification Physical verification of inventory is
a substantive procedure that provides evidence about the existence and condition
of inventory. Therefore, this option is incorrect.
The fourth option: Perform Analytical Procedures Analytical procedures involve
evaluations of financial information through analysis of plausible relationships
among both financial and non-financial data. Comparing inventory turnover ratios
before and after the system upgrade might highlight issues that warrant further
investigation, but this procedure alone does not provide a thorough assessment of
the system’s design and operating effectiveness. Therefore, this option is
incorrect.
source_doc: ACCA:aa:objective:AA Pre Mar 25 Mock Answers (per practice platform).pdf#17

stem_zh:
14. Existence
This is the correct answer. External confirmations from customers primarily
address the existence assertion because they provide evidence that the
receivables recorded in the books of Carlos actually exist.
Completeness: This option is incorrect. Completeness is more about ensuring that
all transactions that should have been recorded have been recorded. In the
context of receivables, completeness would be better addressed by procedures
such as reviewing subsequent cash receipts.
Valuation: This option is incorrect. While they confirm the existence of the
receivable, they do not necessarily provide evidence about its recoverability.
Valuation would be more directly addressed by procedures such as assessing the
allowance for doubtful accounts.
Cut-off: This option is incorrect. The cut-off assertion is about ensuring that
transactions and events have been recorded in the correct accounting period.
Receivables are an account balance and cut-off is for classes of transactions.
source_doc: ACCA:aa:objective:AA Pre Mar 25 Mock Answers (per practice platform).pdf#18

stem_zh:
15. Compare the current year’s depreciation expense with prior years’ expenses and
industry benchmarks to identify any significant fluctuations.
This comparison can provide insights into whether the depreciation policy is
reasonable and consistent with industry practices.
While the other options are valid audit procedures, they do not directly assess the
reasonableness of the depreciation policy. The first option verifies the accuracy of
the recorded cost and useful life, but it does not assess whether the straight-line
method is appropriate. The third option is more about verifying the existence and
condition of the assets, and the fourth option is more related to the maintenance of
the assets rather than their depreciation.
Suggested solution - Jambo Co.
Key Tip: Part (a) is a straightforward knowledge question. Notice that the question asks
for the importance of planning. Answers, which just state what activities are performed
at the planning stage, will not score marks. As planning is the most important stage of
the process, students should be aware of the reasons why it is important to plan the
audit.
(a) Importance of Audit Planning
• It helps the auditor to devote appropriate attention to important areas of
the audit.
• It helps the auditor to identify and resolve potential problems on a timely
basis.
• It helps the auditor to properly organise and manage the audit
engagement so that it is performed in an effective and efficient manner.
• It assists in the selection of engagement team members with appropriate
levels of capabilities and competence to respond to anticipated risks and
the proper assignment of work to them.
• It facilitates the direction and supervision of engagement team members
and the review of their work.
• It assists, where applicable, in the coordination of work done by experts
(b) Audit Risk & Responses
Audit Risk Auditor’s Responses
Jambo Communications Co (Jambo) is a
new client for Poa & Co.
As the team is not so familiar with the
accounting policies, transactions and
balances of Jambo, there will be an
increased detection risk on the audit.
Poa & Co. should ensure they have a
suitably experienced team. In addition,
adequate time should be allocated for
team members to obtain an
understanding of the company and the
risks of material misstatement.
The finance director of Jambo is planning
to capitalise the full $2.2 million of
development expenditure incurred.
However in order to be capitalised it must
meet all of the criteria under IAS 38
Intangible Assets.
There is a risk that some projects may not
reach final development stage and hence
should be expensed rather than
capitalised.
Intangible assets could be overstated and
expenses understated leading to a risk of
overstatement of profits.
Obtain a breakdown of the development
Expenditure from the respective invoice
and review and test in detail to ensure
that only projects which meet the
capitalisation criteria are included as an
intangible asset within the asset register,
with the balance being expensed.
Jambo purchases goods from a supplier
in Central Africa and these goods are
shipped to the company’s central
warehouse. The goods are usually in
transit for two weeks and the company
correctly records the goods when
received.
There is a risk that goods may have been
recorded yet they are still in transit or well
received in the following year leading to
cut-off issues with inventory, purchases
and payables.
There is also a risk of inventory been
overstated because of receiving goods
that have not been received.
The audit team should undertake detailed
cut-off testing of goods in transit from the
suppliers in Central Africa by taking a
sample of inventory received and
recorded in the inventory sheet one week
before and one week after the year-end
and agreeing them to their respective
GDNs to ensure that the cut-off is
complete and accurate.
Manufacturers regularly bring out new
models of mobile phones. When this
happens, the old models have to be sold
at a significant discount, as customers
usually want the latest model. Jambo has
a number of older models in inventory.
The fact that there are many old models
and they have to be sold at a discount
means that the NRV of inventory will
reduce. In accordance to IAS 2 Inventory,
inventory should be valued at the lower of
cost and NRV. There is a risk that
inventory has been valued at cost and not
NRV and hence leading to a risk of
overvalued inventory.
From a sample of old models recorded,
obtain their sales invoices to determine
the NRV.
Obtain the purchase invoices from the
same sample to determine cost.
For the items sampled and tested,
Confirm whether they were recorded at
the lower of cost and NRV or not in the
inventory listing.
Management was disappointed with 20X4
results and hence undertook strategies to
improve the 20X5 trading results.
There is a risk that management might
feel under pressure to manipulate the
results through the judgments taken.
There is a risk of material misstatement of
the financial statements to improve the
trading results
Throughout the audit, the team will need
to be alert to this risk. They will need to
carefully review judgmental decisions and
compare treatment against prior years
through analytical procedures of the
amounts.
A generous sales-related bonus scheme
has been introduced in the year.
Increased sales cut-off testing will be
performed through obtaining GDNs for a
sample of sales recorded pre and post
year-end along with a review of post year-
This may lead to sales cut-off errors with
employees aiming to maximise their
current year bonus.
There is a risk that sales are overstated.
end sales returns as they may indicate
cut-off errors.
As market conditions are difficult for their
customers, they have extended the credit
period given to them.
Receivables may not be recoverable.
There is a risk that receivables are
overstated.
Extended post year-end cash receipts
testing and a review of the aged
receivables ledger to be performed to
assess valuation.
The directors have each been paid a
significant bonus and separate disclosure
of this in the financial statements is
required by local legislation.
The directors’ remuneration disclosure
will not be complete and accurate if the
bonus paid is not disclosed in accordance
with the relevant local legislation.
Discuss this matter with management and
review the disclosure in the financial
statements to ensure compliance with
local legislation.
During the year, whilst calculating
depreciation, the directors extended the
useful lives of plant and machinery from
three years to five years. This reduced
the annual depreciation charge.
Under IAS 16 Property, Plant and
Equipment, useful lives are to be
reviewed annually, and if asset lives have
genuinely increased, then this change is
reasonable.
However, there is a risk that this
reduction could lead to plant and
machinery been overvalued and profit
overstated.
Discuss with the directors the rationale for
extending the useful lives. Also, the five
year life should be compared to how often
these assets are replaced, as this
provides evidence of the useful life of
assets.
The reporting timetable for audit
completion of Jambo Co is quite short,
and the finance director would like to
report results even earlier this year.
Any further reductions will increase
detection risk and place additional
pressure on the team in obtaining
sufficient and appropriate evidence. This
increases detection risk.
The timetable should be confirmed with
the financial director. If it is to be reduced
then consideration should be given to
performing an interim audit earlier, this
would then reduce the pressure on the
final audit.
Jambo Co. provides a two-year repairs
warranty to its customers on its phones of
which the finance manager has created a
provision of $0.2m for it. To try to improve
profits, Jambo changed their main
material supplier to a cheaper alternative.
This has resulted in some customers
claiming on their repairs warranties.
If the overall number of people claiming
on the warranty is likely to increase, then
the warranty provision should possibly be
higher.
If the directors have not increased the
level of the provision, then there is a risk
the provision is understated.
Review the level of the warranty provision
in light of the increased level of claims to
confirm completeness of the provision.
(c) Substantive procedures for directors’ bonus
• Obtain a schedule of the directors’ remuneration including the bonus paid and
cast the addition of the schedule and agree it to the total recorded in schedule
to confirm arithmetical accuracy.
• Agree the individual bonus payments from the bank statements to the payroll
records to confirm that they have been recorded.
• For each bonus paid from the bonus schedule, agree the respective amounts
to the cashbook and bank statements to confirm the accuracy of the amount
of each bonus paid.
• Review the board minutes to confirm whether any additional bonus payments
relating to this year have been agreed to confirm additional obligation to pay.
• Obtain a written representation from management confirming the
completeness of directors’ remuneration including the bonus.
• Review any disclosures made of the bonus and assess whether these comply
with local legislation.
(d) Reporting Implications
• The increase in warranty provision required of $0.7 million represents 7.4% of
profit before tax (0.7/9.4 × 100) which is material to the financial statements.
• The financial statements are materially misstated due to understatement of the
warranty provision. This is material but not pervasive.
• The audit opinion should be qualified with the ‘except for’ wording.
• A basis for qualified opinion would be required to explain the misstatement
arising due to understatement of the warranty provision. The basis for qualified
opinion paragraph would explain the financial effect of the issue on the financial
statement.
• The basis for qualified opinion paragraph should be positioned below the opinion
section within the auditor’s report.

Suggested Solution -
Beats Co
(a)
Direct controls are specific controls which are precise enough to address the risk of
material misstatement at the assertion level, for example, performing a monthly
reconciliation of the bank account which is reviewed, and all differences are resolved.
This is an example of a direct control as it ensures the existence and accuracy of the
asset (bank) at the period end.
Reconciliations – Reconciliations compare two or more data elements for example bank
reconciliation statements. If differences are identified, action is taken to bring the data into
agreement. Reconciliations generally address the completeness or accuracy of
processing transactions.
Verifications – Verifications compare two or more items with each other or compare an
item with a policy for example comparing expense with budget or invoice with Goods
Received Note (GRN) and will likely involve a follow-up action when the two items do not
match. Verif ications generally address the completeness, accuracy, or validity of
processing transactions
Physical or logical controls - These include:
• The physical security of assets, including adequate safeguards such as secured
facilities over access to assets and records.
• The authorisation for access to computer programs and data files (i.e., logical
access).
• The periodic counting and comparison with amounts shown on control records (for
example, comparing the results of cash, security and inventory counts with
accounting records).
Segregation of duties - Assigning different people the responsibilities of authorizing
transactions, recording transactions, and maintaining custody of assets.

Suggested Solution – Autumn  & Co
(a) Red Co – Receivables
• Select a sample of transactions from the sales ledger and inspect the sales
invoice, GDN and sales order to verify  existence.
• Review correspondence with Plain co to verify whether any communication has
been sent in regards to the recoverability of amount.
• Review post year end bank statements to confirm whether any recovery has been
made against this amount after year end.
• Obtain a written representation from Red Co management to confirm the existence
and recoverability to the Plain Co receivable.
• Review minutes of board meetings to assess if directors have discussed the
recoverability of Plain Co receivable and whether they plan on writing the debt off.
(b) Spring Co – Reorganisation
• Review the board minutes where the decision to reorganise the business was
taken, ascertain if this decision was made pre year end.
• Review the announcement to shareholders in late May, to confirm that this was
announced before the year end.
• Obtain a breakdown of the reorganisation provision and confirm that only direct
expenditure from restructuring is included.
• Review the expenditure to confirm that there are no retraining costs included
• Cast the breakdown of the reorganisation provision to ensure correctly calculated.
• For the costs included within the provision, agree to supporting documentation to
confirm validity of items included.
• Obtain a written representation confirming management discussions in relation to
the announcement of the reorganisation.
• Review the adequacy of the disclosures of the reorganisation in the financial
statements to ensure they are in accordance with IAS 37 Provisions, Contingent
Liabilities and Contingent Assets.
(c) Summer Co – Depreciation
• Review the reasonableness of the depreciation rates applied to the new plant and
machinery and compare to industry averages.
• Review the capital expenditure budgets for the next few years to assess whether
there are any plans to replace any of the new plant and machinery, as this would
indicate that the useful life is less than 10 years.
• Discuss with management the assumptions underlying changes in useful lives of
plant and machinery and assess reasonableness of those assumptions
• Select a sample of plant and machinery and recalculate the depreciation charge
to ensure that the non-current asset register is correct.
• Perform a proof in total calculation for the depreciation charged on the equipment,
discuss with management if significant fluctuations arise.
• Review the disclosure of the depreciation charges and policies in the draft financial
statements.
(d)
• Review any bank correspondence to assess the likelihood of the bank renewing
the overdraft facility.
• Discuss with the directors whether they have contacted any alternative banks for
finance to assess whether they have any other means of repaying the bank
overdraft.
• Review the company’s post year-end sales and order book to assess if the levels
of trade are likely to increase in future.
• Review minutes of board meetings to assess whether directors have a plan to
replace suppliers with strict credit terms.
• Review cash flow forecast and evaluate the reasonableness of assumptions to
ascertain whether PharmCo will be able to repay the substantial overdraft.
• Perform sensitivity analysis on profit or loss forecast to assess the impact of
increased interest rate on the profitability of the company.
• Check company’s post year end interim management accounts to verify the impact
on production of the change in supplier terms.

Syllabus Tag: E. Review and Reporting. E1: Subsequent Events. E2: Going
Concern. E5: The Independent’s Auditor’s Report
Case Study: Samsingsong Corporation
The following information relates to question 6-10.
It is 1 July 20X5. You are an audit manager at Seven Kings & Co responsible for the
audit of Samsingsong Co (Samsingsong) for the year ended 30 April 20X5.
Samsingsong is a manufacturing company that produces electronic gadgets. The audit
is almost complete, and the financial statements are due to be signed shortly.
On 1 May, 20X5, Samsingsong received notice of a lawsuit from a competitor alleging
patent infringement. The potential financial impact is uncertain, but it is likely to be
material. The management, after consulting with their legal counsel, has decided to
disclose the lawsuit in the notes to the financial statements as the outcome is possible,
not probable, that they might lose in the lawsuit. This has been confirmed by the audit
team through direct confirmation from the client’s lawyers.
On 15 May, 20X5, Samsingsong’s  management discovered a material fraud. The CFO
had been inflating revenue by recording fictitious sales. The fraud had been ongoing for
the past six months and has resulted in significant manipulation of the financial
statements.
This has also led to incorrect decisions being made based on the inflated financial
statements, such as taking on additional debt that the company is now struggling to
service. Legal proceedings have commenced against the employee responsible, and
their final wages have been withheld.
Samsingsong faces financial strain due to declining sales and increased competition.
Their liquidity position is worsening, and they have defaulted on the last three
payments. The board of directors is concerned about the company’s ability to continue
as a going concern.
source_doc: ACCA:aa:objective:AA Pre Mar 25 Mock Questions (per practice platform).pdf#9

 The auditor's report includes a paragraph that outlines auditor’s
responsibilities regarding other information in the report that contains the
audited financial statements
 The auditor's report must include an Emphasis of Matter paragraph that
highlights a matter that has been correctly disclosed in the financial
statements.
 Key Audit Matters are included in the auditor's report of listed companies.

9. Given the management’s decision to only disclose the lawsuit in the notes
to the financial statements without making any provision for potential
liability, what would be the impact on the auditor’s report?
 Issue an unqualified opinion with an emphasis of matter paragraph.
 Issue a qualified opinion using the “except for” wording due to the lawsuit.
 Issue an adverse opinion due to the lawsuit.
 Issue a disclaimer of opinion
source_doc: ACCA:aa:objective:AA Pre Mar 25 Mock Questions (per practice platform).pdf#13

stem_zh:
10. After conducting procedures on going concern indicators like the additional debt
that Samsingsong is struggling to service , the financial strain due to declining
sales and increased competition, and the notice of a lawsuit from a competitor,
the engagement partner has concluded that there is a material uncertainty
relating to going concern that needs to be disclosed in the financial statements.
Which of the following statements regarding the impact of this disclosure
on the auditor’s report is correct?
 An unmodified audit report will be issued if the management correctly and
adequately discloses the material uncertainty relating to going concern in the
financial statements.
 An adverse opinion will be given in the audit report if the management
correctly gives the disclosure in the financial statements as going concern is
a matter that is fundamental to users’ understanding of the financial
statements.
 An unqualified audit opinion will be given if the management correctly and
adequately discloses the material uncertainty relating to going concern in the
financial statements and an Emphasis of Matters (EOM) section will be
added in the audit report.
 An unqualified audit opinion will be given if the management correctly and
adequately discloses the material uncertainty relating to going concern in the
financial statements and a Material Uncertainty Related to Going Concern
(MURGC) section will be added in the audit report.

Case Study: Carlos Co

It is 1 July 20X5. You are an audit supervisor of Roberto & Co, conducting the audit of
Carlos Co (Carlos) for the year ended 30 September 20X5. Carlo operates a number of
hotels providing accommodation, leisure facilities, and restaurants. Several matters
have come to you attention.

leisure facilities at several of its hotels. The company acquired new gym

the company’s competitive edge.

accurately reflects the physical inventory levels.

o Carlos depreciates its non-current assets over their estimated useful lives.
The company uses straight-line depreciation for its buildings, furniture,
and equipment.

["E. Review & Reporting: E5. The independent Auditor’s Report
Case Study: Jambo Co. Questions 16-19
(a) ISA 300 Planning an Audit of Financial Statements provides guidance to auditors.
Planning an audit involves establishing the overall audit strategy for the
engagement and developing an audit plan. Adequate planning benefits the audit of
financial statements in several ways.
Required:
Explain the importance of audit planning.     (5 marks)
(b) Today’s date is 1 July 20X5. Jambo Communications Co (Jambo) is a large mobile
phone company, which operates a network of stores in countries across Africa.
The company’s year-end is 30 September 20X5. You are the audit senior at Poa &
Co. Jambo is a new client and you are currently planning the audit with the audit
manager. You have been provided with the following planning notes from the audit
partner following his meeting with the finance director. The draft financial
statements show a profit before tax of $9.4 million and revenue of $15.1 million.
During the year, the company has spent $2.2 million on developing several new
products.
Some of these are in the early stages of development whilst others are nearing
completion.
The finance director has confirmed that all projects are likely to be successful and
so he is intending to capitalise the full $2.2 million.
Jambo purchases goods from a supplier in Central Africa and these goods are
shipped to the company’s central warehouse. The goods are usually in transit for
two weeks and the company correctly records the goods when received. Jambo
does not undertake a year-end inventory count, but carries out monthly continuous
(perpetual) inventory counts and any errors identified are adjusted in the inventory
Section B
This section of the exam contains three constructed response questions, all of
which are compulsory.
Each question contains a scenario which relates to one or more requirement(s)
which may be split over multiple question screens.
One question is worth 30 marks and the other two are worth 20 marks each. This
exam section is worth 70 marks in total.
system for that month. Manufacturers regularly bring out new models of mobile
phones. When this happens, the old models have to be sold at a significant
discount, as customers usually want the latest model. Jambo has a number of
older models in inventory.
Jambo’s management were disappointed with the 20X4 results and so in 20X5
undertook a number of strategies to improve the trading results. This included the
introduction of a generous sales-related bonus scheme for their salespersons and
a high profile advertising campaign. In addition, as market conditions are difficult
for their customers, they have extended the credit period given to them.
During the year, the directors have each been paid a significant bonus, and they
have included this within wages and salaries. Separate disclosure of the bonus is
required by local legislation.
During the year, whilst calculating depreciation, the directors extended the useful
lives of plant and machinery from three years to five years. This reduced the
annual depreciation charge.
The reporting timetable for audit completion of Jambo Co is quite short, and the
finance director would like to report results even earlier this year.
Jambo Co. provides a two-year repairs warranty to its customers on its phones of
which the finance manager has created a provision of $0.2m for it. To try to
improve profits, Jambo changed their main material supplier to a cheaper
alternative. This has resulted in some customers claiming on their repairs
warranties.
Required:
(b) Describe EIGHT audit risks, and explain the auditor’s response to each risk,
in planning the audit of Jambo Co.          (16 marks)
Audit Risk Auditor’s Response
(c) Describe substantive procedures you should perform to confirm the
directors’ bonus payments included in the financial statements.       (4 marks)
(d) The audit of Jambo Co. is nearly complete and the auditor’s report is due to be
signed next week. Your audit work discovered that the warranty provision of $0.2m
should be increased to $0.9m because of the increase in claims from customers.
The directors are refusing to make this adjustment.
Required:
Discuss the issue and describe the impact on the auditor’s report if the issue
remains unresolved.               (5 marks)
(30 Marks)
Scenario 2
Syllabus reference: C1, C3 and C4 – Questions 20-21
This scenario relates to two requirements.
(a) ISA 315 (REVISED SEPTEMBER 2019) Identifying and Assessing the Risks of
Material Misstatement states that controls are embedded within the components of
the entity’s system of internal control. Controls may be direct or indirect.
Required:
Define direct controls and describe three examples of direct controls other than
authorisation.               (5 marks)
(b) It is 1 July 20X5.You are the audit supervisor at Sound & Co and are in the process
of reviewing narratives on transaction cycles at Beats Co (Beats) along with
developing audit programmes for the upcoming final audit for the year ending 30
September 20X5.  Beats manufactures and sells a wide range of portable music
players. Beats sells to individual customers via website and also sells to retailers
through a network of sales staff.
Purchase and Goods Receipt
The production department at Beats works on a just -in-time basis. Orders for
necessary materials are dispatched by the computer system according to pre -set
re-order levels. The approved suppliers were all set up in the system two years ago
as a part of improvements in the integrated software.
Deliveries are made within 12 hours by the suppliers who invoice electronically when
goods are dispatched. Items are put into production within hours of arriving at the
Beats central warehouse.
The team at the warehouse check the goods received physically to ensure that
goods being put into production are of appropriate quality or else production will be
held up. The goods receipt note (GRN) is then produced mentioning the time of
delivery and th e quantity of goods delivered and a copy of these GRNs is sent to
accounts and one copy is maintained at the warehouse.
Sales and Dispatch System
Beats’s website allows individuals to order goods directly, and full payment is taken
in advance. Currently the website is not integrated into the inventory system
therefore inventory levels are not checked at the time when individual orders are
placed.
Beats’s retail customers undergo credit checks prior to being accepted and credit
limits are set accordingly by sales director. These customers place their orders
through one of the members of the sales team, who decides on sales discount
levels.
Goods are dispatched via local couriers; however, they do not always record
customer signatures as proof that the customer has received the goods. Over the
past 12 months there have been customer complaints about the delay between
sales orders and receipt of goods. Beats has investigated these and found that, in
each case, the sales order had been entered into the sales system correctly but was
not forwarded to the despatch department for fulfilling.
Required:
In respect of the internal controls of Beats Co:
(i) Identify and explain FIVE deficiencies;
(ii) Recommend a control to address each of these deficiencies; and
(iii) Describe a TEST OF CONTROL the external auditors should perform to assess
if each of these controls, if implemented, is operating effectively to reduce the
identified deficiency.
Note: The marks will be split equally between each part.
(15 marks)
(20 marks)
Scenario 3
Syllabus reference: D4 and E2 – Questions 22-25
This scenario relates to four requirements.
It is 1 July 20X5. You are an audit supervisor with Autumn & Co responsible for the audit
of several clients, all for the year ended 31 May 20X5.
You have been provided the following additional information for some of the clients that
you are working on.
Red Co – Receivables
Red Co has a material receivable balance owing from its customer, Plain Co. During the
audit, your team reviewed the ageing of this balance and found that no payments had
been received from Plain Co for over six months, and Red Co would not allow this balance
to be circularised. Instead, management has assured your team that they will provide a
written representation confirming that the balance is recoverable.
Spring Co – Reorganisation
Spring Co operates a chain of health and fitness clubs. The company recently announced
its plans to reorganise its health and fitness clubs. This will involve closing some clubs for
refurbishment, retraining some existing staff and disposing of some surplus assets. These
plans were agreed at a board meeting in May and announced to their shareholders on 29
May. Spring Co is proposing to make a reorganisation provision in the financial
statements.
Summer Co – Depreciation
Summer Co incurred significant capital expenditure during the year on updating
production facilities in its main factory. The finance director has proposed that the new
plant and machinery should be depreciated over ten years using the straight-line method.
Previously, plant and machinery has always been depreciated over 8 years using
reducing balance method.
(a) Describe substantive procedures to obtain sufficient and appropriate audit
evidence in relation to the valuation of receivables of Red Co.
(5 marks)
(b) Describe substantive procedures to obtain sufficient and appropriate audit
evidence in relation to the reorganisation provision at Spring Co.
(4 marks)
(c) Describe substantive procedures to obtain sufficient and appropriate audit
evidence in relation to the depreciation of Summer Co.
(6 marks)
(d) Autumn Co. is also currently conducting the audit of a prominent client for the year
ending 31 March 20X5. PharmCo is an established pharmaceutical company and has
lately seen a real growth in the level of competition and demand for its products has
significantly declined. PharmCo needed to invest $7m in plant and machinery and wanted
to borrow this sum but was unable to agree suitable terms with the bank; therefore, it
used its overdraft facility, which carried a higher interest rate. Consequently, som e of
PharmCo’s suppliers have been paid much later than usual and some of them have
withdrawn credit terms, meaning the company must pay cash on delivery. As a result of
the above the company’s overdraft balance has grown substantially.
Explain the audit procedures that the auditors should perform in assessing
whether or not PharmCo is a going concern.
(5 marks)
(20 marks)"]

explanation_zh:
本题考查的是audit sampling（审计抽样）的定义和运用。
审计抽样是将审计程序运用到小于100%的样本中，所有的样本都有机会抽中，以使审计师针对样
本总体得出的结论具有合理依据。
审计抽样可以采用统计抽样方法或非统计抽样方法。它涉及到测试较少的项目，并使用结果得出关于整
个余额或交易类别的结论。
审计抽样对于审计师而言，使很有必要的。由于耗时太长成本太高，审计师不可能对所有样本进行测试。
此外，审计师对于财务报表的审计并没有提供100%的保证，只提供了合理的保证，因此没有必要
对样本总体中的每一项都进行测试。
本题总共3分，audit sampling（审计抽样）的定义最高分2分，运用最高分2分。答对一点得1分，最
多3分。属于背诵记忆类的题目。
知识点标签：Other examinable points-Audit procedures and sampling-Audit sampling
难易程度：低

stem_zh:
2、2014 D Section A Q2
答案：D
explanation_zh:
该题考查的是自愿披露和法定披露的例子，考生应当记住法定披露的四种情形， 分别为：洗
钱、策划恐怖袭击、贩毒及法定程序强制要求的。从而我们可以得出（1）和（4）为法定披露。（2）
为非政府组织要求披露和（3）涉及公众利益需要进行披露的都属于自愿披露。题目问的是哪些是自
愿披露，故正确答案为D。该题为单项选择题，答对计 2 分，答错或不答不计分。
知识点标签：Audit and relevant concepts -Professional ethics and quality control
procedures-Fundamental principles of professional ethics
难易程度：低
内部资料，仅供交流学习

stem_zh:
3、2014 D Section A Q9
答案：A
explanation_zh:
审计师在了解到公司内控系统之后需要对系统进行记录，文字描述、问卷调查都属于记录方法。
不同的记录方法有各自的优缺点。这道题考察的是考生对内控记录方式的缺点辨析。
由于问卷调查是审计师在事前统一设计的，所以没有办法避开对某公司的不相关问题，客户在填
写问卷调查的时候可能会遇到大量和自己不相关的问题，而产生困惑。所以 A 选项是缺陷。
相比于其他的记录方式，问卷调查不太容易一楼关键内控的调查，填写起来也比较快。所以 B 选
项和 C 选项都不是内控缺陷。
这道题是 2014 年的 3 选 1，属于简单的选择题，选择正确得 1 分。
知识点标签：Cycles of internal control -Internal control-Procedures of internal control
难易程度：低

stem_zh:
4、2015 J Section A Q7
答案：C
explanation_zh:
UK公司治理规则指出，没有一个director可以制定自己的薪酬，因此C选项中all是错误的表
述是错误的，因此本题的答案为C。
知识点标签：Audit and relevant concepts -Corporate governance-Codes of corporate governance
难易程度：低

stem_zh:
5、2015 J Section A Q11
答案：B
explanation_zh:
本题考查的是internal auditor（内部审计师）和external auditor（外部审计师）的相关概念和辨析。
A 选项Internal auditors are required to be members of a professional body（内部审计师需要是专业团体
的成员）是错误的，并没有这个规定。
C 选项 External auditors report to those charged with governance（外部审计师向治理层汇报）是错误的。
External auditor（外部审计师）向shareholder（股东）汇报。
D 选项 Internal auditors can never be independent of the company（内部审计师在公司里永远不可能保
持独立）是错误的。例如，可以将internal audit function（内部审计业务/功能）外包。
B 选 项 是 正 确 的 ， Internal auditors’ scope of work should be determined by those charged with
governance（内部审计师的工作范围应该由治理层确定）。
知识点标签：Other examinable points-Internal audit-Distinction between internal and external audit
内部资料，仅供交流学习

6、2015 J Section B Q2(a)
答案：
explanation_zh:
这道题是默写内控系统的5个要素。
控制环境是公司上层的行为和态度决定的公司内部的氛围。
风险评估流程是企业在决定把内控设置在哪里提前做出的评估。
信息系统是公司内部在沟通的过程中所有的人，沟通途径，沟通后保留下来的记录或者文件。
控制活动就是公司设置的政策程序，以便于公司能够最后达到目标的措施。监督活动是管理层确
保内控实施的后续措施。
注意，列出名称是 identify的行为，正确列出一个为 0.5分，给出定义是 explain的行为，为0.5分。只有 2个写
全了，才会给完整的1分。
知识点标签：Cycles of internal control -Internal control-What is internal control system
难易程度：低
内部资料，仅供交流学习

explanation_zh:
这道题考察的是考生对于审计证据质量高低的概念默写题。来自外部的证据明显比来自内部的证
据质量高。
来自好内控公司得证据比来自坏内控公司得证据质量高。审计师直接获取证据得质量要比审计师
间接获取质量高。书面证据要比口头证据质量高。
原始的文件要比影印件来的质量高。
列举出4个比较因素即可获得满分。写bullet point不得分，需要一句完整的句子。
知识点标签：Audit planning and risk assessment --Audit evidence

stem_zh:
9、2016 M/J Section B Q1
答案 1：
explanation_zh:
本题考查公司治理的缺陷及建议，每个缺陷一分，每个建议一分，这道题是满分 10 分，
所以建议要根据自己的时间，至少写出 5 个。
缺陷：根据 Codes of corporate governance（公司治理准则），非执行董事人数应占董事会成员人数
的一半或以上，而此案例中，执行董事与非执行董事的配比是不合理的（备注 1），这样的情况极
有可能会造成一家独大或霸权的局面。
建议：董事会，非执行董事人数至少应占董事会成员人数的一半。所以对于 Tangerine 公司，应当
考虑增加 NED（非执行董事）的人数以满足要求
知识点标签：Audit and relevant concepts -Corporate Governance-Codes of corporate governance
难易程度：中
内部资料，仅供交流学习
- 8 -
答案 2：
解析：
缺陷：根据 Codes of corporate governance（公司治理准则） ，非执行董事是需要独立于公司管理
的，而 Tangerine 公司的 Chairman（董事会主席）与其中一个 NED（非执行董事）在退休之前均是
公司的执行董事，受聘于公司，参与公司运营管理，是不够独立客观的；而其他的 NED（非执行
董事）的独立性，也没有进行考虑（备注 2）。
建议：Tangerine 公司的董事会，非执行董事应具备独立性以及公司业务相关的经验与能力。所以，
Tangerine 公司的非执行董事的独立性应进行评定，如果有不符合的成员，应另找符合要求的人员
进行替换。
知识点标签：Audit and relevant concepts -Corporate Governance-Codes of corporate governance
难易程度：中
答案 3：
解析：
缺陷：根据 Codes of corporate governance（公司治理准则），审计委员会的成员均为独立的非执行
董事，而审计委员主席，对于小规模企业来说，如果董事会主席是独立的非执行董事，可以由董
事会主席来担任。但是作为像 Tangerine 公司潜在上市公司来说，由董事会主席来担任审计委员主
席（备注 3），是不符合准则的。
建议：董事会主席不可以担任审计委员主席。所以，应另选独立的非执行董事来担任审计委员主
席。
知识点标签：Audit and relevant concepts -Corporate Governance-Codes of corporate governance
难易程度：中
答案 4：
内部资料，仅供交流学习

解析：
缺陷：根据 Codes of corporate governance（公司治理准则），审计委员会的成员至少有一位成员具
备近期相关的财务经验。而 Tangerine 公司审计委员会的成员的经历均与财务无关（备注 4）。
建议：审计委员会的成员应至少有一位成员具备近期相关的财务经验，所以公司应招聘一位新的
具备要求的独立的 NED（非执行董事）加入审计委员会。

答案 5：
解析：
缺陷：根据 Codes of corporate governance（公司治理准则），对于董事会成员任期是有规定，每三
年要进行一次选举，而 Tangerine 公司董事会成员任期都超过了 4 年（备注 5），这是不恰当的。
建议：根据规定，董事会成员任期不得超过三年。Tangerine 公司要在当年的股东大会上，对于任
期满三年的成员进行再选举。未满三年的，下一年要进行评定。
知识点标签：Audit and relevant concepts -Corporate Governance-Codes of corporate governance
难易程度：中
答案 6：
解析：
缺陷：根据 Codes of corporate governance（公司治理准则），与股东们沟通，应于董事会成员全员
参与，而 Tangerine 公司只有董事会主席负责沟通（备注 6），这是不恰当的。
建议：董事会成员全员参与与股东们沟通。Tangerine 公司，董事会应在年度报告中进行说明，为
内部资料，仅供交流学习

确保董事会成员，特别是非执行董事，了解大股东对公司的看法而采取了哪些步骤。
知识点标签：Audit and relevant concepts -Corporate Governance-Codes of corporate governance
难易程度：中
答案 7：
解析：
缺陷：审计委员会要关注公司内控的有效性，内审部门应进行支持性工作，而现在 Tangerine 公司
并没有设立内审部门（备注 7），因此，审计委员会因每年考虑是否需要建立内审部门。
建议：Tangerine 公司要考虑是否成立内审部门。因为内审部门可以帮助审计委员会进行内控监督。
同时还要考虑成本问题，是否符合公司效益。
知识点标签：Audit and relevant concepts -Corporate Governance-Codes of corporate governance
难易程度：中

explanation_zh:
本题在第一小问的基础上考查考生对银行余额这个科目可以实施的具体审计程序的理解，要求考
生举例说明。注意，本小问是要求写例子。
检查：检查银行对帐单中是否有在途存款，并核对到年前cash book，年后bank statement
以及年前paying-in-book
观察：观察被审计单位员工打开邮件和记录从客户那里收到的任何支票的过程，以确保有充分不
兼容职务分离。
分析性程序：分析企业今年年底的银行余额与去年相比的变动情况，找到有异常波动的地方并判断是否
存在财报造假是否需要与管理层讨论。
询问：问管理层所审计年份是否有新增或关闭银行账户。

重新计算：重新算一下年末现金日记账的增加额确保年底的数字是准确的。
询证函：向被审计单位当年有使用的所有银行发询证函获取审计证据。重新执行：重新做一遍银
行余额调节表以确保其结果是准确无误的。
每个例子 1 分，最多 5 分。

stem_zh:
14、2016 S Section B Q16(b)
(b) Identify and explain SEVEN deficiencies in the sales system of Heraklion Co and provide a
recommendation to address each of these deficiencies.
Note: Prepare your answer using two columns headed Control deficiency and Control recommendation
respectively. (14 marks)
Control deficiency Control recommendation
答案 1：
内部资料，仅供交流学习
- 18 -
explanation_zh:
销售人员定新客户的赊销额度，会导致销售人员为了自己的销售业绩目标而过于简单地设置赊销
额度，从而产生不良信用风险。
Recommend：一般来说，销售人员不能设定赊销额度。但是如果是由销售人员设定的赊销额度，则需要
有信用调查机构对信用设定作检查。一旦设定好的信用额度经过授权， 则需要信用控制官将数据输入系
统，以便未来销售时的使用。而设定好的信用额度不是一成不变的，需要定期复核调整。
内控缺陷的识别0.5分，解释0.5分，一个有效recommend为1分。
知识点标签：Cycles of internal control -Test of controls-The sales system
难易程度：中
答案 2：
解析：销售人员有权利自行决定提供给客户的、幅度在10%以内的折扣，会导致销售人员为了自己的销售
业绩目标而尽可能给客户提供较高水平但不切实际的折扣，如果没有其他人对该折扣进行复核的话，会
使企业损失收入。
Recommend：所有对客户批准的折扣都需要事先经过相关负责人（比如销售总监）的授权，确保
所有提供给客户的折扣都经过检查，都是恰当的。
内控缺陷的识别 0.5 分，解释 0.5 分，一个有效 recommend 为 1 分。
知识点标签：Cycles of internal control -Test of controls-The sales system
难易程度：中
答案 3：
解析：销售人员在master file中输入记录销售折扣可能会有两个问题：一个问题是输入的结果可能会
内部资料，仅供交流学习
- 19 -
不正确，而导致企业开票过高，多收客户的钱，影响客户商誉，或开票过低，使企业损失收入；另一个问
题是普通的销售人员级别不够高，不能由他对去接触master data file，也不能由他对master data file做调
整更改，否则可能会产生master data file中产生未经授权的折扣，而导致舞弊的问题。
Recommend：首先普通的销售人员不能负责在master data file中输入、调整、修改折扣信息，而需要
supervisor等级别较高的人员才能进入系统并进行修改。其次如果是人为修改的，还需要有另一个责
任人对修改作复核。
内控缺陷的识别0.5分，解释0.5分，一个有效recommend为1分。
知识点标签：Cycles of internal control -Test of controls-The sales system
难易程度：中
答案 4：
解析：销售人员直接拜访客户，并签订订单，但在之前并没有先确认存货是否充足，是否可获得。这会导
致企业接受的订单因为没有可以发的货，而无法完成订单，影响企业商誉。
Recommend：在接受客户订单之前是必须要先对存货系统进行检查的，以保证企业有足够的存货
能够提供给客户。
内控缺陷的识别0.5分，解释0.5分，一个有效recommend为1分。
知识点标签：Cycles of internal control -Test of controls-The sales system
难易程度：中
答案 5：
解析：销售订单一式两份，分别发给客户和销售人员自留，而不发给仓库和财务部门， 也不放在销售
部门内保存，会导致：仓库接受到的发货信息可能存在差错，而发错货或发错人；财务部门没收到，则无
法得知这笔销售发生了，就无法及时记录账务处理；销售人员自己保存会容易丢失，使销售部门有留底，无
内部资料，仅供交流学习
- 20 -
法备查，也无法对自身的销售情况作监督。这些都会使企业的收入和客户商誉产生负面影响。
Recommend：销售订单至少需要一式四联，分别发给销售部门、客户、财务部门和仓库。而销售部门需要定期
复核订单对GDN的匹配，以便发现是否存在未完成的订单。
内控缺陷的识别0.5分，解释0.5分，一个有效recommend为1分。
知识点标签：Cycles of internal control -Test of controls-The sales system
难易程度：中
答案 6：
解析：销售订单是以销售人员自己的ID号来编号的，所以只要是同一个销售人员签订的订单，它们的
编号将会全部一致，很明显这会导致销售订单没有连续编号。而不连续编号的订单，会使丢失的订单无
法及时发现追踪并完成，最终影响企业商誉。
Recommend：销售订单需要连续编号，并且对于所有连续编号的单据都需要有定期复核单据是否连续
完整，以便及时识别丢失的单据。
内控缺陷的识别0.5分，解释0.5分，一个有效recommend为1分。
知识点标签：Cycles of internal control -Test of controls-The sales system
难易程度：中
答案 7：
解析：销售人员将订单上的细节信息以邮件的形式发给仓库部门，可能会产生邮件里的销售内容和销售
订单上的不一致，或邮件里的销售内容遗漏了一些重要信息。这些差异或遗漏都会导致发错货或发错人，
最终影响企业收入和商誉。
Recommend：如本题答案 5所述，销售订单至少需要一式四联，其中一联需要发给仓库。而仓库产生的发货清
单需要基于原始订单上的信息而产生，通过检查确保发货的数量、质量、产品规格等都是满足订单要求
的。
内部资料，仅供交流学习
- 21 -
内控缺陷的识别0.5分，解释0.5分，一个有效recommend为1分。
知识点标签：Cycles of internal control -Test of controls-The sales system
难易程度：中
答案 8：
解析：连续编号的GDN生成后直接在仓库归档，不给财务部门提供一联，会让财务部门不知道什么时
候货物已经发出，也不知道什么时候能够确认销售收入，会导致延迟确认收入，销售收入被低估的可能。
Recommend：GDN至少需要一式四联，分别发给销售部门、客户、财务部门和仓库自留。当GDN发
给财务人员后，财务人员需要将GDN和订单进行核对，并以此信息开票，保证开票的准确性。
内控缺陷的识别0.5分，解释0.5分，一个有效recommend为1分。
知识点标签：Cycles of internal control -Test of controls-The sales system
难易程度：中
答案 9：
解析：公司的赊销期限是30天，但是销售人员有责任对账龄超过90天的应收账款进行追款。这意味着这些
应收账款本身已经大大超过了企业设定的赊销期限，导致企业的现金流入时间被推迟。同时，因为销售人
员自身的业绩要求，所以他们会跟关注订单是否能签下来，而并不那么在意订单是否会回款。这也会导致应收
账款的可回收性大大降低， 可能会使应收账款坏账的增加，进一步减少企业利润和现金流入。
Recommend：一般会由信用控制官关注并监督应收账款的回收情况，因此在本题中就需要设立这
样一个职位，以保证其能及时发现那些账龄超过30天的应收账款。
内控缺陷的识别0.5分，解释0.5分，一个有效recommend为1分。
知识点标签：Cycles of internal control -Test of controls-The sales system
难易程度：中
内部资料，仅供交流学习

(c) In relation to the payroll fraud, identify and explain THREE controls Heraklion Co should
implement to reduce the risk of this type of fraud occurring again and, for each control, describe
how it would mitigate the risk. (6 marks)
Control Mitigate risk
答案 1：
解析：在题干中有描述道：存在大量虚假的员工被建立了档案并被支付了工资。为了保证企业的员工
都是真实员工，所以在建立员工档案的时候要注意进行身份验证，这个验证是需要留下证据的，确保只
有真实的员工才能够被建立员工档案，并且这些新建立档案的基础信息比如员工姓名等只能有人力资源
部门的人员去操作。并且从题干中科院得知企业是有人力资源部门，这个控制是可行的。
Mitigate risk：由于需要验证核查，需要保留核查证据，相对来说流程变得更加复杂繁琐，也加大
了建立虚假员工档案的难度，从而减少舞弊风险。
控制的识别0.5分，解释0.5分，一个有效减少风险方式为1分。
知识点标签：Cycles of internal control -Test of controls-The payroll system
难易程度：高
答案 2：
解析：同样针对的是存在大量了虚假的员工被建立档案的情况，比较直接的应对方式就是数人数，每个
部门分别统计各自部门的人数，与系统中的员工总数进行比对。
Mitigate risk：如果实际员工人数和系统人数之间有差异，则可能出现错差或者存在舞弊，特别是系统人数大
于实际员工人数则说明有可能存在虚假员工，需要进一步调查。
控制的识别0.5分，解释0.5分，一个有效减少风险方式为1分。
知识点标签：Cycles of internal control -Test of controls-The payroll system
难易程度：高
内部资料，仅供交流学习
- 23 -
答案 3：
解析：在题干中有描述道：建立新员工档案的人员是属于薪酬部门的，换句话说就是， 如果是薪酬
部门的人员建立新员工档案就更容易建立虚假员工而产生舞弊，因为没有人知道到底企业增加了多少员
工，是不是真正增加的。因此如果能让新员工先填表，并分别提交给具体的部门、人力资源部门和薪酬部门，
则会有更多人了解新员工新增的情况。
Mitigate risk：这里更多的体现的是权责分离的问题，也就是建立完整地新员工档案并不只包括单独的一
个部门，人力资源部门需要先更新基础信息，薪酬部门再根据基础信息更新员工薪酬信息，两个部门能互
相牵制，从而减少虚假员工的可能性。
控制的识别0.5分，解释0.5分，一个有效减少风险方式为1分。
知识点标签：Cycles of internal control -Test of controls-The payroll system
难易程度：高
答案 4：
解析：在答案 3中已经提到了，新员工档案的建立是从人力资源部门开始，只有接收到了人力资源部门
发来的信息后，薪酬部门才能以此为基础，添加薪酬信息。这里要强调的是，添加薪酬信息的这一动作是
需要经过授权的，也就是不是薪酬人员能够随意修改或调整的。同时，添加和调整结束了也需要有相关
的复核。
Mitigate risk：授权对系统的调整能让更多的人知道系统的变化，很大程度上减少了私自调整而产
生舞弊的风险。
控制的识别0.5分，解释0.5分，一个有效减少风险方式为1分。
知识点标签：Cycles of internal control -Test of controls-The payroll system
难易程度：高

解析：在题干中描述了两个人的工作职责，本来他们之间满足权责分离的，但是问题在于这两人结婚了是
夫妻。在这种情形下，其实很难保证他们不会联合起来规避内控程序。因此，为了保证能够比较好的执行权责分离，
应该要尽可能的避免员工之间的关联关系。
Mitigate risk：避免员工之间的关联关系，从而减少员工的合谋舞弊。

答案 6：
解析：正常的工资发放的银行账户所有权人和银行账户应该都是不一样，每个人的工资分别都能发到自
己的银行账户中。而题目中提到了所有虚假员工的工资全部发到了一个相同的银行账户中。如果在发放工
资前，对发放清单中的员工姓名、银行卡号作一个检查，就很容易能够发现这个异常。
Mitigate risk：通过对支付对象、支付账户作一个检查，能及时发现重复的账户，从而及时发现可能的舞
弊异常。
控制的识别0.5分，解释0.5分，一个有效减少风险方式为1分。
知识点标签：Cycles of internal control -Test of controls-The payroll system
难易程度：高
答案 7：
解析： 在题干中说道，一个监督人员负责授权银行转账支付的同时，还负责银行转账， 这就会产生没有权
责分离的问题。自己授权，自己执行，其实质就是没有授权。因而， 如果他要做银行转账这一行为，就
需要有更高层的相关负责人对这一支付进行授权。
Mitigate risk：在分层级授权的时候还需要检查各项支付信息，比如答案6中提及的银行账户信息，以保
证没有支付异常，减少舞弊可能性。
控制的识别0.5分，解释0.5分，一个有效减少风险方式为1分。

explanation_zh:
此题考查的是对于固定资产科目的实质性审计程序问题，但和常见题型的不同点在于， 此题在设计审
计程序时不是从认定的角度出发，而是从题目所给出的事项出发。题目中描述，被审计客户在当年度进
行了固定资产的重新估值，并聘请了估值专家完成这一事项。那我们在考虑答案时，就要从以下几个方面
入手，包括为什么要进行估值调整，估值专家的胜任能力，专家的估值方法和假设是否合理，重新估值结
果计算是否准确，以及对资产后续计量的影响等角度设计实质性程序。
每步审计程序能得到1分，所以共须写出5步审计程序。

此题考查的是对于存货的估值问题，根据会计准则的要求，存货应按照成本及可变现净值熟低来计价，

stem_zh:
16、2017 M/J Section B Q17(c)
答案：
explanation_zh:
本题考查的知识点是银行余额的实质性程序，是考生一定要掌握的知识点。
-从Airsoft Co的银行家那里获得其所有银行帐户的银行询证函。
-将银行询证函上所有的账户核对Airsoft Co的银行对帐单和试算平衡表表的以确定其银行余额的
准确性。
-获取所有银行帐户的余额调节表并重新计算以确保算术准确性。
-将银行余额调节表上的银行存款余额核对到银行对账单和询证函上。
-将银行余额调节表上的cash book余额核对到现金日记账上。
-对于在途存款，查年前cash book年后bank-statement以及年前paying-in-book
-对于未承兑汇票，查年前cash book年后bank statement。对于一些异常或大额的，则要去询问管理层-获取
其解释。
-检查账龄长的未承兑汇票是否已冲销。
-检查年末现金日记账和银行对账单上是否存在异常或大额交易，这很可能是舞弊迹象。
-检查询证上上是否有抵押担保或其他需要披露的相关事项。
-检查财报确定其恰当披露。
写对每一条得1分，最多5分。
知识点标签：Obtaining the audit evidence -Cash and bank-Audit plans for bank
难易程度：高
内部资料，仅供交流学习

17、2017 S/D Section B Q16(a)(b)(d)
答案：
explanation_zh:
本题考查的是面对利益冲突时可以采用什么方法。题干中 Halley 公司同时审计了 Comet Publishing
公司和 Edmond 公司，这两家公司互为竞争对手，因此会产生利益冲突。会计师事务所首先需通知
双方公司，并且获取双方的同意、建议一个或两个客户寻求额外的独立建议、使用不同的项目团
队、严格隔离双方团队等措施。
每个措施为 1 分，考生需写出 5 条措施才能获得满分。

environment
难易程度：中
答案：
解析：
这道题考察的是审计如果要了确认自己对内控系统的理解是否正确，应该采用哪些步骤。这道题
考法确实刁钻，需要考生对整体的流程把握非常深入。

stem_zh: 1.审计师首先要获得上一期关于该公司采购系统记录的文件，看看我们了解的是否完整。

2.复核审计底稿，查看是否有显而易见的内控缺陷，并且今年对此类缺陷的调查信息和结果都需要
记录在案。

3.查看上一年给管理层提供的管理层报告中，给管理层提供的内控改进管理层是否已改。针对已经
内部资料，仅供交流学习
- 29 -
改进的部分今年要重点核查。
4.获取客户的系统文件查找最近 12 个月额外的内控改动。

5.和客户进行面谈，了解包括仓库、店铺在内的内控活动是否有发生过更改，记录和去年流程图上
的差异之处。
6.实施穿行测试确定审计师目前手头的关于公司采购系统的内控流程图和文字描述是准确的。

7.在执行穿行测试的过程中确认流程图和文字表示准确的反应了现实情况。
1 个有效的步骤为 1 分。
知识点标签：Cycles of internal control -Internal control-Procedures of internal control
难易程度：高
答案：
explanation_zh:
本题考查可以对purchase and other expenses实施的实质性审计程序，我们可以采用如下实质性审计
程序：
-计算营业利润和毛利率，并将其与去年和预算进行比较，并调查任何重大差异。
-检查每月的采购和其他支出，一旦发现任何重大波动就与管理层讨论。
-与管理层讨论主要供应商是否发生任何变化，并将其与采购分类账进行比较，以评估采购的完整
性和准确性。
-重新计算采购发票总额和相关税款以确认其准确性。
-重新计算年末收取的预付款项和应计费用，以确保损益表中包含的费用支出的准确性。
-选择一个年后的发票检查确保（不是应该记录在年前的）与审计当期相关的都已经记录在年前。
-从现金簿中选择付款样本并追溯到费用科目，以确保其正确记账并正确分类。
-从全年的收货通知单（GRN）中抽取样本核对到采购发票和采购日记账以确认收入的完整性。
-从年底前后的GRN中抽样核对到采购日记账以确定其记录在正确的会计期间。
内部资料，仅供交流学习
