not have gained the available 1 mark. When listing these types of detailed tests, the
key point when the sale should be recognised is when the goods have been
dispatched and so tests should begin or end with the GDN rather than the sales order
or invoice. Take the time to read the question requirements carefully and spend time
thinking about what is needed prior to producing an answer.
It is also important to ensure the procedures have sufficient detail and are clear. For
example, ‘perform a cut-off test’ without describing what the actual test is and what
documents should be used will not gain any marks. The source documents are GDNs
before and after the year end, and these need to be agreed to sales invoices and
traced into the detailed sales listing to ensure they have been included in the correct
accounting period. If sales invoices had been used as the source document rather
than GDNs then only ½ mark would have been awarded.
Requirement (e) – 4 marks
(e) Describe TWO corporate governance deficiencies faced by Granstan Co and
provide a recommendation to ensure compliance with corporate governance
principles.
For corporate governance questions, candidates are generally asked to identify and
explain a number of corporate governance deficiencies from a given scenario and give
a relevant recommendation to ensure compliance with corporate governance
principles.
Candidates are awarded ½ mark for identifying the deficiency and ½ mark for
explaining the implication of the deficiency. Candidates are awarded 1 mark for each
well-explained recommendation.
Examiner’s report - AA September/December 2024 15
It was pleasing to see that most candidates were able to identify the deficiencies from
the scenario. Having identified the deficiency, the next step is to clearly explain the
implication of the deficiency. Candidates often fail to do this and miss out on the ½
mark available. The explanation needs to be clear on how this results in a corporate
governance issue. For example, just stating that ‘this is poor corporate governance’
would not be awarded credit.
The final step is to then suggest a recommendation to ensure compliance with
corporate governance principles and it is important that this is phrased as an action;
often candidates provide objectives rather than actions. Also, candidates must ensure
that t he recommendation will fully address the deficiency. For example, for the
deficiency relating to the CEO becoming the Chair, a recommendation of ‘do not
appoint the CEO as Chair’ will not be awarded credit, as it does not resolve the issue
of Granstan Co not having a relevant Chair. In order to gain the one mark available,
the recommendation must give a clear action, for example ‘a new Chair who is
independent of Granstan Co should be appointed’.
Additionally, some candidates did not provide sufficient detail in their recommendation
for the deficiency of all remuneration being set by the CEO. Responses needed to
specify who should set the remuneration for executive and/or non-executive directors
(NEDs). In order to gain the one mark available, the recommendation should be ‘NEDs
remuneration should be set by the board’ or ‘executive’s remuneration should be set
by a remuneration committee made up of independent NEDs’. Simply stating that the
‘executive directors should be set by a remuneration committee’ would only gain ½
mark as it does not state the composition of the committee.
Good questions to practice the skill of describing corporate governance deficiencies
and making relevant recommendations include Petra Co from the Sample March/June
2023 exam, Daley Co from September/December 2022 and Freesia Co from
March/June 2019.
Examiner’s report - AA September/December 2024 16
Musitastic Co
This 20-mark question is based on Musitastic Co, which sells musical instruments and
offers weekly lessons from 50 stores across the country. This question tests
candidates’ knowledge of the benefits of audit planning and audit risks and responses.
Requirement (a) – 4 marks
(a) Explain the benefits of audit planning.
This is a relatively straightforward knowledge requirement which has been tested in
previous exam sessions. Knowledge requirements such as this often have an opening
statement, sometimes referenced to an auditing standard, and this is useful for setting
the scene and providing clarification on the aim of the question requirement.
Also, when reviewing knowledge requirements, it is important to identify whether an
understanding of the scenario is required in order to attempt the question. Where the
knowledge requirement is not linked to the scenario a note is included confirming this.
Candidates should be aiming to score full marks for knowledge questions, therefore it
is especially important that they understand exactly what the question is asking.
Question requirements such as this demonstrate the importance of having a detailed
understanding of the ISAs, and in this case ISA 300 Planning an Audit of Financial
Statements. For a four-mark knowledge requirement such as this, candidates should
aim to provide four well-explained points.
Care should be taken when reading the requirement to ensure that answers focus on
the correct issues. For example, in this session some candidates incorrectly focused
their answers on determining the audit approach, gaining an understanding of the
client o r considering the audit timetable and/or budget which were not relevant.
However, generally, most candidates performed well on this requirement.
Requirement (b) – 16 marks
(b) Describe EIGHT audit risks and explain the auditor’s response to each risk
in planning the audit of Musitastic Co.
Marks are awarded for identification of audit risks (½ mark each), explanation of audit
risks (½ mark each) and an appropriate auditor’s response to each risk (1 mark each).
With a scenario-based requirement such as this, good exam technique is critical. The
scenario will typically contain more than the number of risks required, so it is important
that candidates plan their time carefully and only attempt to list the required number
of points.
Examiner’s report - AA September/December 2024 17
The first step is to identify the factors which will give rise to an audit risk. This
information can be found in the scenario. All of the information in the scenario should
be read carefully, including the opening paragraph as this may include information
relevant to the identification of audit risks, such as that Musitastic Co is a new client.
When reading through the information, it is good exam technique to use the highlight
function as this provides a visual aid for quickly spotting audit risks. Having looked at
the whole scenario and highlighted relevant points, candidates should pick their eight
strongest points, re- read them from the scenario, and draft their answer as they go
along.
Candidates often use the copy and paste function when identifying risks. When doing
this, care should be taken to ensure that the risk is actually identified. Simply stating a
fact from the scenario is not always the same as identifying an audit risk.
Financial accounting knowledge is also important as audit risks will often focus on the
accounting treatment used in the financial statements. In Musitastic Co, accounting
issues which give rise to audit risks include those relating to revenue recognition,
property plant and equipment, and loans and provisions. Hence a strong knowledge
of the relevant accounting standards is required to fully understand the audit risk and
generate a relevant auditor response.
Candidates should expect a range of topic areas within an audit risk scenario, some
of which may be more challenging than others. In this exam session, it was pleasing
to see that candidates were able to confidently identify a good cross section of the
risks. Candidates should also note that when the scenario states ‘the company
obtained an $8m interest -bearing bank loan which is repayable in quarterly
instalments, in arrears, over five years’, this information does not generate two
different audit risks. In this session, some candidates identified the risk of classification
of the loan and interest not being accrued as two separate audit risks, however credit
was only given once for either of these points. Candidates should note that one piece
of information in the scenario will generally only be credited for one audit risk.
Having identified the risk factor, the next step is to explain the risk. To do this,
candidates need to state the specific area of the financial statements impacted with
an assertion (for example cut -off, valuation, etc.), or, a reference to
over/under/misstated, or, a reference to inherent/control/detection risk. ‘Misstated’ will
only be awarded if it is clear the balance could be either over or understated. For
example, if the risk should have been described in terms of an understated balance,
then no credit would be awarded if candidates referred to a misstated balance.
Candidates cannot hedge their bets by providing both options.
The explanation of the risk must clearly state the specific area of the financial
statements impacted. For example, in respect of the issue relating to the refurbishment
of the stores, only noting ‘non-current assets or assets could be overstated’ would not
Examiner’s report - AA September/December 2024 18
be awarded credit. An appropriate explanation in this instance would be ‘property,
plant and equipment could be overstated’.
In addition, some risk explanations were inappropriate. For example, for the risk
relating to reliance on internal audit, few candidates correctly explained that there was
a detection risk if the work undertaken by internal audit was irrelevant or poorly
performed. For the risk relating to advertising expenditure being recognised as a
current asset, some candidates incorrectly focused on how the expenditure should be
an intangible asset rather than fully expensed to profit or loss. For the new loan, some
candidates incorrectly believed that needing a loan meant the company was facing
going concern issues. This is not the case and so no credit was awarded. Candidates
must take the time to carefully read the scenario, noting any relevant information, to
ensure they correctly understand and describe the audit risks arising.
Having identified and explained the risk, the next step is to provide the auditor’s
response. Responses must be practical within the context of the scenario and care
should be taken to ensure the response is that of the auditor, and not a management
response.
In this session, many candidates provided management responses for the audit risk
relating to the retirement  of the financial controller, such as ‘recruit a replacement
financial controller.’ However, a replacement had started in March 20X5, and the
auditor focus should have been over the five-month period whilst there was no financial
controller. Candidates are advised to pause and read their responses to ensure that
what they are recommending will help the auditor form a conclusion on the identified
audit risk.
In this session some candidates struggled to produce valid auditor responses for the
risk relating to the inventory count over three days. Candidates focused on the cut-off
of inventory pre and post year end, rather than on the adjustments made for inventory
movements between the count days and year end. Some candidates incorrectly stated
that all inventory movements should be stopped, or that the count should only be over
one day, or incorrectly focused on the valuation of inventory.
Additionally, a significant minority of candidates gave general statements of the
required accounting treatment. For example, for the revenue recognition risk, it was
common to see ‘all income should be recognised as deferred income’ rather than how
the auditor would test whether revenue had been recognised for lessons delivered by
the year end. This general statement approach indicates a lack of practice in tackling
different risks and responses questions from previous exams.
Candidates often suggest ‘increased professional scepticism’ for a whole range of
audit risks, and whilst valid, it is not on its own a suitable auditor’s response for 1 mark.
This is because increasing scepticism does not, on its own, help the auditor to gain
Examiner’s report - AA September/December 2024 19
suitable audit evidence over the identified audit risk. For the risk of cash differences
between the till and records, simply stating ‘increase professional scepticism’ will only
gain ½ mark. To gain the full mark, candidates need to focus on additional substantive
testing of cash balances or discussing with management the need to investigate the
differences. This was the only risk which gained credit for ‘increased professional
scepticism’, it was not a valid response for any of the other audit risks.
Auditor responses do not have to be a detailed procedure, rather it is an approach the
audit team will take. Care must be taken however, to ensure that the approach
suggested addresses the risk identified and contains sufficient detail. A response of
‘discuss with management’ will not gain any credit as candidates need to be very clear
exactly ‘what’ they are ‘asking management’ about. Where further documentary
evidence is available to the auditor, candidates need to refer to this to gain the
available 1 mark per response. Also, consideration should be given to the reliability of
audit evidence gained; for example, evidence gained via confirmation from a third
party, such as lawyers, will be more reliable than verbal assertions from management.
Future candidates are advised that audit risk is, and will continue to be, an important
element in the syllabus and must be understood. Candidates must ensure that they
include adequate question practice as part of their revision on this key topic.
Good scenario-based audit risk questions to practice from published sample exams
are: Green Co March/June 2024, Knight Electronics Co September/December 2023,
Lapis Co March/June 2023, Magpie Co September/December 2022, Esk Co
March/June 2022, Peach Co September/December 2021, Corley Appliances Co
March/June 2021, Hart Co September/December 2020, Scarlet Co March/June 2020,
Harlem Co September/December 2019 and Peony Co March/June 2019.
Examiner’s report - AA September/December 2024 20
Tennessee & Co
This 20-mark question is based on Tennessee & Co, an audit firm due to commence
the audit of two existing clients, Montana Co which is a not -for-profit charitable
organisation, and Oklahoma Co which manufactures aircraft components. This
question tests candidates’ knowledge of audit procedures in relation to income,
inventory counts and work in progress, and auditor’s reports.
Requirement (a) is for 5 marks, (b) for 6 marks and (c) for 4 marks and time allocation
should be based on 1.8 minutes per mark. Therefore, the available time should be split
at 9 minutes for requirement (a), 11 minutes for (b) and 7 minutes for (c).
One mark is available for each well-explained procedure, therefore candidates should
aim to produce the same number of procedures as there are marks. Candidates must
strive to understand substantive procedures and apply good exam technique. This
includes tailoring procedures to the specific requirements of the question. Too often
candidates have rote learnt a set of standard tests and these are then produced for
each requirement without consideration of their relevance to the scenario provided.
This approach is unlikely to generate many marks.
Audit procedures must be sufficiently detailed noting clearly which source document
should be used. For example, tests such as ‘review disclosures’ would only score ½
mark. To score a full mark the procedure should go on to say, ‘in accordance with
accounting standards/relevant legislation’.
Requirement (a) – 5 marks
(a) Describe substantive procedures the auditor should perform to obtain
sufficient and appropriate audit evidence in relation to Montana Co’s income.
The key point to note for this requirement is the nature of the entity and the balance
being audited. This question requires substantive procedures in relation to income for
a not -for-profit charitable organisation. The charity’s main sources of income are
monthly donations, ticket sales for two charity events held during the year and sales
of merchandise at these events. Spending time understanding the nature of Montana
Co and carefully reading the scenario ensures that any procedures listed are tailored
and more likely to score marks.
Producing a list of rote- learned procedures, especially those related to a
manufacturing company, will not gain credit, therefore testing sales invoices and
goods dispatched notes (GDN) which may be valid in other circumstances, would not
be relevant here.  In this session many candidates included procedures such as
agreeing income to sales invoices and GDNs, recalculating sales invoices, and
Examiner’s report - AA September/December 2024 21
performing cut-off procedures using GDNs around the year end. These procedures
would not have gained credit as they were not relevant for the charity described in the
scenario. In addition, many candidate’s procedures included reviewing the gross profit
margin, even though the charity is a not-for-profit organisation.
Analytical procedures are an important source of evidence when auditing revenue and
there are a number of examples which would be relevant in this case, for example,
comparison of different categories of income with the prior year and investigation of
any significant differences.
In this question, information is given about the sale price of tickets for the charity
events and the approximate number of people expected to attend. This information
can be used to describe a detailed proof in total procedure. Additionally, there are
sales of merchandise, and this can also be used in an analytical comparison to
previous charity events.
Careful thought should be given as to why information has been included in the
scenario and how it can be used to make procedures as specific as possible. We are
told that tickets are sold in the four months leading up to the event, there is an event
in Ap ril and Montana Co’s year end is March. The scenario also suggests that
completeness and cut -off of income are key risks. This information is there to help,
trying to encourage candidates to think about these assertions and focus their
procedures accordingly. The treatment of the advance ticket sales for the April event
would be a key consideration for the auditor to confirm that the income has been
recorded in the relevant accounting period. However, very few candidates considered
the cut-off for the ticket sales and the treatment of deferred income.
Candidates must ensure that they can distinguish between a substantive procedure
and a test of control. Many candidates lose marks in this type of requirement by mixing
up these procedures. For example, in this session some candidates provided test of
controls to ensure the controls over cash collection were operating effectively; these
are not substantive procedures.
Procedures which gained credit included casting the income and agreeing to the trial
balance and financial statements, tracing monthly donations to the bank ledger and
bank statements, agreeing merchandise sales to the bank ledger, and analytical
review procedures including proof in total.
As discussed, many candidates incorrectly provided generic revenue procedures,
however many also listed procedures over cash and bank, presumably because some
of the merchandise sales were for cash. Other procedures which failed to gain credit
included obtaining a written representation and reviewing disclosures.
Candidates must ensure they practice generating substantive procedures for different
account balances. They should be confident that, when faced with an unfamiliar
Examiner’s report - AA September/December 2024 22
account balance or type of organisation, they can apply first principles and use the
scenario provided to generate sufficient substantive procedures.
Requirement (b) – 6 marks
(b) Describe the audit procedures the auditor should perform as part of the audit
of Oklahoma Co BEFORE and DURING the inventory count.
In common with part (a) it is important to carefully consider the requirement and review
the scenario to understand the issue arising. In this case, the scenario indicated that
a full year -end inventory count would be undertaken at the company’s factory and
warehouse. In addition, the scenario stressed that there would be significant work in
progress (WIP) and that there would be no movements of goods in or out of the
warehouse during the count.
The requirement strictly focused on procedures both before and during the count and
although not required, it would be useful to split the procedures into before and then
during the count as this helps to ensure a good cross section of points are covered
and reduces overlap and repetition of points.
Unfortunately, many candidates did not read the requirement carefully and therefore
listed procedures which would be undertaken after the count at the final audit e.g.
reviewing aged inventory records for slowing- moving goods or reviewing post -year-
end sales invoices to assess NRV. These procedures would not gain any credit as
they were not relevant to the requirement. In addition, those candidates who provided
valuation procedures in part (b) often then failed to list these in requirement (c) which
was for  valuation, thereby compounding the issue. Candidates must read all
requirements before starting their answer, as it is unlikely that two requirements will
test the same area.
Procedures before the count which will gain credit are those which relate to
understanding the risks associated with the inventory count such as reviewing the prior
year audit file and discussions with management concerning control issues in the
factory or  warehouse. In addition, inventory count instructions would need to be
reviewed in detail to identify whether any control deficiencies may arise, as these
would need to be discussed in advance of the count. Candidates who simply stated
‘obtain inventory count instructions’ would not have gained credit as simply obtaining
them is not undertaking an audit procedure, they need to be reviewed. As work in
progress is significant then understanding exactly how the percentage of completion
will be assessed is key.  Some candidates incorrectly focused on identifying which of
the company’s warehouses should be visited despite the scenario clearly stating there
was only one factory and warehouse.
Examiner’s report - AA September/December 2024 23
Moving onto procedures during the count, it is important to understand that the role of
the auditor is to test whether the controls over the count are functioning correctly, to
obtain information for the final audit such as obtaining a copy of the completed
inventory count sheets, and to undertake detailed testing to confirm the completeness
and existence of the inventory records. In addition, work in progress needs to be
assessed and the reasonableness of any assumptions used needs to be considered.
A significant minority of candidates incorrectly believed that procedures during the
inventory count referred to during the final audit and so focused on testing valuation
of inventory. These did not gain any credit.
In testing the controls during the count, a key procedure would be to observe the
counters to ensure the inventory count instructions are being followed correctly.
Having gained credit for this procedure some candidates then laboured this point by
listing various elements of the instructions which should be confirmed e.g. observe the
counters are flagging inventory once counted. These candidates listed several controls
in the count process which should be confirmed or observed which did not gain any
further marks. In addition, many candidates incorrectly provided statements of what
controls should be in place within the count, e.g. inventory should be counted in teams
of two. This did not gain credit as it is not an audit procedure.
Candidates who considered whether the inventory count was being undertaken in line
with the instructions, undertook test counts between the warehouse floor and records
and confirmed the operation of procedures over work in progress, controlling
movement of goods, and identification of damaged inventory gained sufficient credit.
Requirement (c) – 4 marks
(c) Describe substantive procedures the auditor should perform during the final
audit to obtain sufficient and appropriate audit evidence in relation to the
VALUATION of Oklahoma Co’s WORK IN PROGRESS.
As noted above it was disappointing that some candidates incorrectly provided
valuation procedures for part (b) and then failed to list them in part (c). The scenario
for this requirement detailed that the company manufactured aircraft components and
that at the year end there would be a significant amount of work in progress (WIP).
Additionally, the WIP is calculated by applying an estimated percentage completion to
the standard cost of a completed component. It is important to consider the nature of
the issue contained in the scenario and then compare it to the question requirement.
Some of the key words in this requirement are in capital letters. Here, it is clear that
the substantive procedures must only focus on whether WIP is VALUED appropriately
at the year end. Spending time understanding the issue and carefully reading the
Examiner’s report - AA September/December 2024 24
requirement ensures that any procedures listed are tailored and more likely to score
marks. As the requirement is for valuation, the procedures need to focus on confirming
the cost of WIP, specifically how the standard cost is calculated and the basis for
determining the percentage of completion. When assessing the net realisable value
(NRV) in order to compare against cost, as the requirement is only for WIP, costs to
complete must also be reviewed. Any tests relating to finished goods would not be
credited. Lastly it is important to appreciate that as the requirement only relates to the
valuation assertion, any general inventory wide procedures such as cut-off testing are
not relevant and so would not gain any credit.
When generating the substantive procedures, a sensible approach would be to first
consider how to verify each element of standard cost. A procedure such as agreeing
the raw material costs to recent purchase invoices would have confirmed the cost for
1 mark. Similar well-explained procedures for labour costs and production overheads
would each have generated additional marks. Moving on to the percentage of
completion, agreeing the percentage completion to the year -end inventory count
records for a sample of WIP would gain another mark.
When considering NRV, it is important to ensure that the tests provided are focused
and detailed. Procedures such as ‘confirm inventory is valued at lower of cost and
NRV’ or ‘carry out NRV tests’ will not gain credit as there are no details as to how this
will be done. Valid analytical procedures such as compare the WIP balance to the prior
year and investigate any significant differences would gain 1 mark. However,
calculation of inventory holding days would not gain credit as it is based on total
inventory rather than WIP.
In this session those candidates who included procedures such as recalculate WIP
based on stage of completion, discuss the basis of the WIP with management, agree
the standard labour costs to payroll records, review the overheads included to ensure
they are of a production nature, and compare the WIP balance to the prior year and
investigate significant differences, gained credit. Candidates who included procedures
such as ‘review whether the disclosure is in accordance with accounting standards,’
or ‘obtai n written representations’ did not gain credit. Requesting written
representation should be restricted to areas where the auditor is relying on
management’s judgement or where there is little independent evidence available. This
is not the case with valuat ion of WIP and tests must be tailored to fit with the
requirement and scenario.
Good examples to practice from published sample exams are: Cookit Co March/June
2024, Latte Co September/December 2023, Heron Co March/June 2023, Pacific Co
September/December 2022, Spinach Co March/June 2022, Danube Co
September/December 2021, Purrfect Co March/June 2021, Sagitarrii & Co
September/December 2020, Encore Co March/June 2020, Spadefish Co
Examiner’s report - AA September/December 2024 25
September/December 2019, Hyacinth Co March/June 2019, Jasmine Co
September/December 2018, and Gooseberry Co March/June 2018.
Requirement (d) – 5 marks
(d) Discuss the issue and describe the impact on the auditor’s report, if any,
should this issue remain unresolved.
Although auditor’s reports feature regularly in the AA exam, there are several ways in
which they can be tested, and candidates must be prepared for any type of question.
This session the issue which needed to be considered related to the application of an
incorrect percentage of completion when valuing work in progress, and management’s
decision not to adjust for this overstatement of $0.7m. Auditor’s report questions have
shown a gradual improvement in recent sessions and performance for this question
was satisfactory.
Marks are awarded for a discussion of the issue (1 mark), assessment of the
materiality of the issue (1 mark), a description of the type of modification (up to 2
marks) and the resultant impact on the auditor’s report (1 mark).
In order to be awarded the mark for discussing the issue, candidates should read the
scenario carefully and consider exactly why a modification may be necessary.
Candidates needed to explain that the percentage completion calculation for the work
in progress contained errors (½ mark), and that inventory and profit would be
overstated and cost of sales understated if not adjusted (½  mark). Recent sessions
have seen an improvement in candidates’ discussion of the issue, and this continued
this session, however, more candidates only mentioned the overstatement of inventory
and failed to mention the incorrect percentage of completion calculation.
Candidates were able to gain the ½ mark for correctly calculating materiality and a
further ½ mark for correctly assessing that this was material. Also, candidates
satisfactorily described the type of modification and the impact on the auditor’s report.
When considering the type of modification candidates are reminded that no credit is
awarded for qualified ‘report’ or ‘the audit report is modified’ as it is the opinion, rather
than the report, which is modified. Some candidates continue to waste time by li sting
all possible options in a scatter gun approach as seen in previous sessions, as well as
referring to emphasis of matter or key audit matters, which are irrelevant.
Auditor’s reports are a core area of the syllabus and knowledge of the ISAs in this area
is imperative. Good questions to practice from published sample exams are: Cookit
Co March/June 2024, Pacific Co September/December 2022, Spinach Co March/June
2022, Danube Co September/December 2021, Purrfect Co March/June 2021,
Sagitarrii & Co September/December 2020, and Encore Co March/June 2020."]

stem_zh:
3. 写1-2条针对风险给出的应对措施    (1mark) Tips: 可以参考下方模板中给到的思路进行书写，注意不要写成给管理层提建议。
模板使用提示： 1. 本文件中给出的必背模板覆盖了90%考试中常见常考的知识点，建议考生确保吃透这些知识点基础上，通过练习历年真题再补充其余冷门考点。 2. 下方清单是自2016年9月以来的常见考点，供考生自查掌握情况。 PPE 1. Initial Measurement 2. Capital & Revenue Expenditure 3. Depreciation 4. Disposal Intangible Asset 1. Capital Expenditure - R&D 2. Amortization Inventory 1. Valuation - Cost vs NRV (存货周转天数上升) 2. Inventory Count 3.Overseas purchase Trade Receivables 1.  Allowance for receivables (应收账款周转天数上升) Bank Loan 1. Classification of Liabilities 2. Interest Expenses Trade Payables 1. Control deficiencies/Completeness (应付账款周转天数减少) Provision 1. Redundancy 2. Litigation  Equity 1. Shares Issue Sales 1. Deferred Income 2. Sales Return 3. Sales-Related Bonus Scheme Purchase 毛利率净利率变动不一致 Detection risk 1. New Client 2. Time pressure 3. Reliance on Internal Auditor 4. Outsourcing 2 Others 1. Planning to undertake Stock Exchange Listing (公司计划上市)  Tips：上述PPE至Sales都直接跟报表科目挂钩，response部分与后面其他风险的答题思路有区别，需要重点背诵。
资产类科目考试中被高估的情况较多
负债类科目考试中被低估的情况较多
PPE 1. Initial Measurement （高频必会） Audit risk Auditor response 考点：只有使得资产达到预定可使用状态的成本才能资本化，包含采购成本及必要的安装检查成本(installment cost, preparation of the site, testing costs, legal costs)；培训费、维修费、管理费等都不符合条件；若被资本化，资产和利润被高估，费用被低估。 题目背景中将不满足条件的费用计入了PPE. （0.5） Only directly related costs of bringing asset to its working condition should be capitalized. XX costs should not be capitalized.（解释原因） Incorrect accounting treatment may cause profits and PPE overstated, and expenses understated. （结论）（0.5）
source_doc: ACCA:aa:objective:1-24年11月更新-高顿ACCA AA主观题得分要点-全文背诵.pdf#1

stem_zh: 1.Obtain a breakdown of capitalized costs and agree to supporting documentation to ensure that they meet the recognition criteria.（必背） 2.Discuss the accounting treatment with the directors to ensure appropriate treatment of the XX costs.  建议写一条与管理层讨论的程序+一条获取书面文件确认的程序 2. Capital vs Revenue Expenditure （高频必会） Audit risk Auditor response 区分清楚资本化支出及费用化支出，不符合资本化性质的，如果被资本化进资产，会导致资产和利润被高估，费用被低估。 题目中存在一笔资产维修/更新成本.客户全部计入PPE。（0.5） According to IAS 16, only the expenditure of a capital nature should be capitalized. (解释原因) If not correctly allocated between revenue and capital expenditure, PPE and profit could be overstated, and expense could be understated. （结论）0.5
source_doc: ACCA:aa:objective:1-24年11月更新-高顿ACCA AA主观题得分要点-全文背诵.pdf#2

stem_zh: 1.Obtain breakdown of these costs and assess the nature and further testing should be undertaken to ensure that the classification is correct. 2.Discuss with management about the accounting treatment, and assess the reasonableness.  建议写一条与管理层讨论的程序+一条获取书面文件确认的程序 3. Depreciation （高频必会） Audit risk Auditor response 随意增加固定资产使用年限，折旧费用降低。存在高估资产和利润，低估费用的风险。 Or PPE已报废但还未折旧完全，说明折旧政策不合理，导致高估资产、低估费用。 题目中提到了修改折旧年限/obsolete资产的折旧还没计提完. （0.5） Under IAS 16 PPE, depreciation policy should be reviewed annually, and the change should be reasonable. (解释原因) If is not appropriate, PPE and profits may be overstated and expense understated. （结论）0.5
source_doc: ACCA:aa:objective:1-24年11月更新-高顿ACCA AA主观题得分要点-全文背诵.pdf#3

stem_zh:
1. Discuss with management the rationale of depreciation policy and assess reasonableness. 2. Recalculate the depreciation and compare to how often these assets are replaced. 折旧政策属于管理层的估计，建议写一条与管理层讨论合理性的程序+一条重新计算和对比数据合理性的程序 4. Disposal （高频必会） Audit risk Auditor response 资产处置时存在重大损/益，可能计算有误，也可能因为折旧政策不合理，导致前期折旧费用有误，CV不准确。
题目中提到处置资产时出现重大亏损(0.5)  Significant profits or losses on disposal may be due to the incorrect calculation ,and also an indication that the depreciation policy may not be appropriate. （解释原因） Therefore, depreciation expense may be understated and PPE and profit may be overstated. （结论）0.5
source_doc: ACCA:aa:objective:1-24年11月更新-高顿ACCA AA主观题得分要点-全文背诵.pdf#4

stem_zh: 1. Discuss with management about depreciation policy and assess its reasonableness.  2. Recalculate the loss on disposal to ensure accuracy. 建议写一条与管理层讨论合理性的程序+一条重新计算确认准确性的程序 Intangible Asset 1. Capital Expenditure - R&D （高频必会） Audit risk Auditor response 研发费用不符合PIRATE条件，但计入了IA，会导致资产和利润被高估，费用被低估。 题目中提到公司在研发新产品/专利或将广告费用确认为无形资产。(0.5) Under IAS 38, only development costs that meet criteria could be capitalised. (解释原因)If research costs have been incorrectly classified, there is a risk that intangible assets could be overstated and expenses understated. （结论）（0.5）
source_doc: ACCA:aa:objective:1-24年11月更新-高顿ACCA AA主观题得分要点-全文背诵.pdf#5

stem_zh: 1. Discuss with the management on the accounting policy to identify the R&D stages. 2. Detailed review on the capitalized costs to identify their nature and agree them to the criteria for capitalization. 能否资本化依赖于管理层的专业判断，建议写一条与管理层讨论合理性的程序+一条获取书面文件确认性质的程序 2. Amortization (类比PPE的折旧) （参照前面写法套路一样） Audit risk Auditor response 摊销政策不合理或者计算有误。存在高估资产，高估利润，低估费用的风险。 题目中提到需要摊销或摊销政策不合理等问题. （0.5） Under IAS 38, intangible asset should be amortized over the useful life. (解释原因) There is a risk that amortization has not been correctly calculated, resulting in misstated expenses. （结论）0.5
source_doc: ACCA:aa:objective:1-24年11月更新-高顿ACCA AA主观题得分要点-全文背诵.pdf#6

stem_zh: 1. Discuss with management and assess the reasonableness of intangible assets’ useful life. 2. Recalculate the amortization to confirm the accuracy. 摊销政策属于管理层的估计，建议写一条与管理层讨论合理性的程序+一条重新计算确认准确性的程序 Inventory 1. Valuation - Cost vs NRV (存货周转天数上升) （高频必会） Audit risk Auditor response 产品损坏、滞销、过时、退货都可能意味着NRV<cost，且存货周转天数增加，但没计提减值，导致存货被高估，费用被低估。 题目中出现product recall、damaged、obsolete、slow-moving这些词汇，如果给了inventory days也要写进来. （0.5） Inventory should be valued at the lower of cost and NRV. （解释原因）Therefore, inventory may be overvalued as its NRV may be lower than cost. （结论）0.5
source_doc: ACCA:aa:objective:1-24年11月更新-高顿ACCA AA主观题得分要点-全文背诵.pdf#7

stem_zh:
1. Discuss with the finance director whether any write downs will be made to the affected products. 2. Perform testing on cost and NRV of the affected products, to ensure the valuation.
是否需要计提减值依赖于管理层的专业判断，建议写一条与管理层讨论合理性的程序+一条审计师自己做减值测试的程序 2. Inventory Count  Audit risk Auditor response 审计师无法参与所有区域的存货盘点，存货可能存在被错报风险。 题目中提到被审计公司有很多地方年末都需要盘点。（0.5） It is unlikely that the auditor could attend all of the counts, auditors may not obtain sufficient appropriate audit evidence. (解释原因) Therefore, it increases detection risk（结论） （0.5）
source_doc: ACCA:aa:objective:1-24年11月更新-高顿ACCA AA主观题得分要点-全文背诵.pdf#8

stem_zh: 1. The auditor should assess for which of the building sites they will attend the counts. 2. For those not visited, discuss with management and review the level of exceptions noted during the count. 建议写一条决定哪些地方需要盘点+一条与管理层讨论没盘点的地方是否存在异常。 3.Overseas purchase （高频必会） Audit risk Auditor response 海外采购且运输时间较长，所以做了规定，当供应商发货时XX Co就需要记账。(正常本应收货记账) 所以如果忘了发货时就记账，导致存货及应付账款都被低估。 题目中提到海外采购且在途时间较长。（0.5） There is a risk that XX Co not correctly records at the point of dispatch, （解释原因）which leads to cut-off issue, while inventory, purchase and trade payables may be understated. （结论）（0.5）
source_doc: ACCA:aa:objective:1-24年11月更新-高顿ACCA AA主观题得分要点-全文背诵.pdf#9

stem_zh: 1. Select samples of shipping documents before and after the year-end and perform detailed cut-off testing.  2. Discuss with management the point that inventory is recorded and review the contract with the supplier to verify the time. 建议写一条与管理层讨论确认收货的时间点+一条审计师执行截止性程序 Trade Receivables 1.  Allowance for receivables (应收账款周转天数上升) （高频必会） Audit risk Auditor response 可能会有账款收不回来，需要计提坏账，可能存在应收账款被高估、费用被低估的风险 题目中出现应收账款周转天数增加、信用负责人离职、没有额外计提坏账准备等情况。（0.5） Some receivables may be irrecoverable and higher level of allowance required. （解释原因） Therefore, may lead to overstated receivables and understated allowance. （结论）（0.5）
source_doc: ACCA:aa:objective:1-24年11月更新-高顿ACCA AA主观题得分要点-全文背诵.pdf#10

stem_zh:
1. Extend post year-end cash receipts testing and a review of the aged receivables ledger, to assess its valuation.  2. Discuss with the finance director the adequacy of any allowance on the old balances. 坏账准备是管理层的估计，建议写一条与管理层讨论坏账准备是否充足+一条期后回款测试/复核账龄的程序 Bank Loan 1. Classification of Liabilities、 Audit risk Auditor response 超过1年的银行贷款，需要在每年末把1年内需要偿还的金额从NCL重分类至CL，否则会导致负债分类有误。
题目中提到了被审计公司借款，且分期偿还. （0.5） There is a risk that the loan has not been correctly allocated between current and non-current liabilities （解释原因）which would give rise to a classification error and liabilities being misstated. （结论）（0.5）
source_doc: ACCA:aa:objective:1-24年11月更新-高顿ACCA AA主观题得分要点-全文背诵.pdf#11

stem_zh:
1.Re-perform the company’s calculations to confirm that the split of the loan note is correct between non-current and current liabilities. 2.Disclosures for this loan note should be reviewed in detail to ensure compliance with relevant accounting standards. 考试中上述程序二选一即可。 2. Interest Expenses （高频必会） Audit risk Auditor response 贷款会相应产生利息，如果忘了计提会导致利息费用被低估，利润被高估。 题目中提到了借款（需要付利息）.  There is a risk that this has been omitted from the statement of profit or loss leading to understated finance costs and overstated profit.
The finance costs should be recalculated and any increase agreed to the loan documentation. Trade Payables 1.Control deficiencies/Completeness (应付账款周转天数减少) （高频必会） Audit risk Auditor response 应付账款不再进行对账、应付账款周转天数减少、对于应付的内控没做好、采购发票漏记等，可能导致应付账款发生错报 (尤其完整性问题，可能被低估)。 题目中提到了跟应付和采购处理错误的情况. （0.5） Weak control leads to increased control risk and risk of misstatements arising. （解释原因） Cost of sales, expenses and trade payables may not be complete or accurate./ Trade payable and purchase may be understated （结论）（0.5）
Detailed testing of the payables balance, and review of supplier statement reconciliations, to ensure all liabilities are recorded. Discuss with management about how the issue arose, and whether have been correctly treated. 如果涉及到内控的再写跟管理层讨论的问题。 Provision 1.Redundancy (高频必会) 公告时间是一个高频考点 Audit risk Auditor response 年前宣布裁员(需支付遣散费) - 存在现时义务，履行该义务很可能导致企业经济利益流出，且金额能可靠计量，需确认预计负债。否则高估利润，低估负债。 题目中出现了redundancy或restructuring重组. （0.5） The timing of this announcement has not been confirmed; if it is announced to the staff before the year end, a redundancy provision will be required at the year end.（解释原因）Failure to do so will result in an understatement of provisions and expenses. （结论）（0.5）
source_doc: ACCA:aa:objective:1-24年11月更新-高顿ACCA AA主观题得分要点-全文背诵.pdf#12

stem_zh:
1. Discuss with management the status of the redundancy announcement; if before the year end, review supporting documentation to confirm the timing.  2. Review the basis of and recalculate the redundancy provision. 预计负债通常涉及管理层的专业判断， 建议写一条与管理层讨论+一条复核文件的程序 2. Litigation （高频必会） Audit risk Auditor response
存在未决诉讼，可能导致赔偿，如果没正确处理会导致预计负债被低估或披露不完整。 题目中提到公司被告或者投诉。（0.5） According to IAS 37, if it is probable that XX Co. will make paymen, a provision for unfair dismissal is required. If the payment is possible rather than probable, a contingent liability disclosure would be necessary.(解释原因) If XX Co.has not done this, there is a risk over the completeness of any provisions or contingent liabilities disclosures. （结论）（0.5）
The audit team should discuss with management and request confirmation from the company’s lawyers of the existence and likelihood of success of any claim from the former financial controller. Tips：一定要看清被审计公司是原告还是被告，上述情况适用于被告的情况，如果是原告，可能收到别人的赔偿，则需要回答contingent asset或有资产披露的情况。 Equity 1.Shares Issue  Audit risk Auditor response 公司发行的股票有溢价，股本与溢价部分需要分开列示，且需有恰当披露。 题目中提到issue shares at premium.（0.5）  Adequate disclosure should be made and the equity finance needs to be allocated correctly between share capital and share premium. （解释原因）If this is not done, then the accounts may be misstated due to a lack of disclosure or share capital and share premium may be misstated.（结论） （0.5）
Confirm that proceeds were received and that the split of share capital and share premium is correct and appropriately recorded. The disclosures for this finance should be reviewed in detail to ensure compliance with relevant accounting standards and local legislation. 考试中上述可以二选一 Sales 1.Deferred Income （难点） Audit risk Auditor response 收取客户定金/提供长期服务，需先确认递延收益(负债)，只有完成履约义务才能确认收入；否则存在高估收入，低估负债的风险。 题目中提到deposit，或 者 提 前 收 取 客 户 资金、提前确认收入等情况. （0.5） These deposits should not be recognised as revenue. Instead should be recognised as deferred income within current liabilities. （解释原因）Management may have overstated revenue and understated liabilities. （结论）（0.5）
Discuss with management the treatment of deposits received in advance, to ensure it is appropriate.  During the final audit, undertake increased testing over the cut-off of revenue and completeness of deferred income. 建议写一条与管理层讨论会计处理+一条增加截止性测试的程序 2. Sales Return （高频必会） Audit risk Auditor response 附有退货条款/价格保证/产品退回的销售，这部分需要计入负债(refund liability)而不能确认收入，否则存在高估收入，低估负债的风险。 题目中提到了销售的退货率、价格保证price promise、销售退回product recall等情况。（0.5） Discuss with management the basis of the refund liability of $xxx and obtain supporting
This will result in XX Co. paying refunds to customers. The sales will need to be removed from the current year FS and a refund liability recognised.（解释原因）This may result in overstated revenue, understated liabilities and misstated inventory. (结论) （0.5）
documentation to confirm the reasonableness of the assumptions and calculations.
source_doc: ACCA:aa:objective:1-24年11月更新-高顿ACCA AA主观题得分要点-全文背诵.pdf#13

stem_zh:
3. Sales-Related Bonus Scheme Audit risk Auditor response 绩效以收入为评估基础，奖金与收入挂钩；可能会通过销售给低信用顾客来提高收入，不仅收入存在被高估风险，还可能导致款项收不回来、以及cut-off问题。 题目中提到销售人员或者管理人员sales-related bonus scheme（0.5） Sales staff seeking to maximise their current year bonus may result in new accounts being opened from poor credit risks leading to irrecoverable receivables. (解释原因) There is a risk of sales cut-off errors. （结论）(0.5)
Increased sales cut-off testing will be performed along with a review of any post year-end returns.  Increased after date cash receipts testing to be undertaken.
Purchase 毛利率净利率变动不一致 Audit risk Auditor response 毛利率增加、净利率下降，且变动幅度较大，可能成本与费用分类有误。 题目中ratio显示gross profit margin较上年增加，operating profit margin下降. （0.5） This movement in gross margin is significant and inconsistent with the fall in operating margin. There is a risk that costs may have been omitted or included in operating expenses rather than cost of sales.  （解释原因）Misclassification of expenses would result in understatement of cost of sales and overstatement of operating expenses.（结论）（0.5）
The classification of costs between cost of sales and operating expenses should be reviewed in comparison to the prior year and any inconsistencies investigated.
Detection risk 1.New Client （高频必会） Audit risk Auditor response 被审计单位是新客户，由于不熟悉，或者因为上期审计不是自己审的，期初数可能不准确，检查风险增加。 题目中提到 new client. （0.5） As the audit team is not familiar with the accounting policies, transactions,（解释原因） there will be an increased detection risk on the audit. （结论） （0.5）
Suitably experienced team assigned to the audit and that adequate time is allowed for team members to obtain an understanding of the company.
source_doc: ACCA:aa:objective:1-24年11月更新-高顿ACCA AA主观题得分要点-全文背诵.pdf#14

stem_zh:
2. Time pressure  Audit risk Auditor response 客户要求提前公布财报/结束审计工作。审计项目时间压力大，无法保证审计程序完整执行。 题目中提到report results earlier. （0.5） A reduction in the audit timetable will increase detection risk and place additional pressure on the team in obtaining sufficient and appropriate evidence. （0.5）
The timetable should be confirmed with the finance director. If it is to be reduced, then consideration should be given to performing an interim audit. 3. Reliance on Internal Auditors  Audit risk Auditor response 外部审计师计划依赖内审工作，如果内审工作质量较差，可能导致外部审计师工作量不够，未获取到充分适当审计证据的情况下出具审计报告，增加检查风险。 题目中提到 place reliance on the controls testing work undertaken by the IA department. （0.5） If reliance is placed on irrelevant or poorly performed testing, then the external audit team may form an incorrect conclusion. （解释原因）This could result in them performing insufficient levels of substantive testing, thereby increasing detection risk. （结论）（0.5）
Before using the work of IA, the audit team will need to determine adequacy of relying on them. In addition, the team will need to re-perform some of the testing carried out by IA to assess its adequacy.

stem_zh: 4.Outsourcing （高频必会） Audit risk Auditor response 外包某些职能，未获取到充分适当审计证据的情况下出具审计报告，增加检查风险。 题目中提到 outsource某个职能（大部分情况考payroll）. （0.5） A detection risk arises as to whether sufficient and appropriate evidence is available to confirm the completeness and accuracy of controls over the payroll cycle and liabilities at the year end.（0.5）

stem_zh:
1. Discuss with management any monitoring of controls which has been undertaken by management over payroll. 2. Consideration should be given to contacting the service organisation’s auditor to confirm the level of controls in place, a type 1 or type 2 report could be requested. 建议写一条与管理层讨论外包控制的程序+一条联系对方审计师并获取相关资料的程序 Others 1. Planning to undertake Stock Exchange Listing (公司计划上市) Audit risk Auditor response 被审计单位计划上市，或者想卖掉股票，因此可能会高估收入、利润、资产等。 题目中提到stock exchange listing或attempt to sell shares. （0.5） Maintain professional scepticism and be alert to the increased risk of manipulation.
There is motivation to manipulate FS, by overstating revenue, profits and assets. （0.5） Significant estimates and judgements should be carefully reviewed in light of the misstatement risk.   二、Internal control专题必背答题模板 1. 内控题目首先看题目中是否要求写covering letter或者write a report to management，如果需要，则要在答题之前书写下方的信件，考试中占2分。 Covering letter Report to management Board of directors XX Co （抄公司名称） DATE 20XX （改为题目背景中的时间） Dear Sirs, Audit of Amberjack Co for the year ended DATE Please find enclosed the report to management on deficiencies in internal controls identified during the audit for the year ended DATE. The appendix to this report considers deficiencies in the sales and dispatch system and recommendations to address those deficiencies. （写信的目的） Please note that this report only addresses the deficiencies identified during the audit and if further testing had been performed, then more deficiencies may have been reported. （明确责任） This report is solely for the use of management and if you have any further questions, then please do not hesitate to contact us. （礼貌结尾） Yours faithfully An audit firm Appendix Control deficiency Control recommendation    2. 常见考法： （1） Key Control + Test of control （找企业做的好的控制，并执行控制测试） （2） Weakness + Recommendation （3） Weakness + Recommendation + Test of control （2）（3）找企业做的不好的地方，解释对企业内控的影响，提出恰当建议，执行控制测试  Deficiency Recommendation Test of control 找对/Copy-0.5 合理解释-0.5 Tips：合理解释一定是落实在对企业内部控制的影响。
1-2条针对性的应对措施 Tips：应对措施一定要具体，且要完整。 1-2条针对性的控制测试 Tips: 控制测试一定要与前面找到的key control或者recommendation相关的。  Tips for Internal control: 内控的三大目标：经营有效、财报真实、合法合规 解释缺陷（答案要落实在下方的解释中才得分）：
